**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"**Borrower**\"), has entered into that certain First Lien Credit Agreement dated as of July 31, 2025 (as amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time, the \"**First Lien Credit Agreement**\"), among the Borrower, the lenders party thereto from time to time (the \"**First Lien Lenders**\"), and Cartwright National Bank, N.A., as administrative agent and collateral agent (in such capacity, the \"**First Lien Agent**\"), providing for a first lien credit facility in an aggregate principal amount of Four Hundred Seventy-Five Million Dollars (\$475,000,000), consisting of (a) a revolving credit facility in an aggregate principal amount of One Hundred Twenty-Five Million Dollars (\$125,000,000) (the \"**First Lien Revolver**\") and (b) a term loan A facility in an aggregate principal amount of Three Hundred Fifty Million Dollars (\$350,000,000) (the \"**First Lien Term Loan A**\");

**WHEREAS**, the Borrower has entered into that certain Second Lien Credit Agreement dated as of July 31, 2025 (as amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time, the \"**Second Lien Credit Agreement**\"), among the Borrower, the lenders party thereto from time to time (the \"**Second Lien Lenders**\"), and Ridgeline Capital Partners, LP, as administrative agent and collateral agent (in such capacity, the \"**Second Lien Agent**\"), providing for a second lien term loan B facility in an aggregate principal amount of One Hundred Fifty Million Dollars (\$150,000,000) (the \"**Second Lien Term Loan B**\");

**WHEREAS**, the total secured indebtedness of the Borrower under the First Lien Credit Agreement and the Second Lien Credit Agreement is Six Hundred Twenty-Five Million Dollars (\$625,000,000) in aggregate principal amount as of the date hereof;

**WHEREAS**, the obligations of the Borrower under the First Lien Credit Agreement and the Second Lien Credit Agreement are secured by liens on substantially all of the assets and property of the Borrower and the other loan parties, and the parties hereto desire to set forth their respective rights, obligations, and agreements with respect to the relative priorities of their respective liens on such Collateral (as defined below) and certain other intercreditor matters; and

**WHEREAS**, it is a condition precedent to the effectiveness of each of the First Lien Credit Agreement and the Second Lien Credit Agreement that the parties hereto shall have entered into this Agreement;

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the foregoing premises and the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties hereto agree as follows:

**[ARTICLE I --- DEFINITIONS; CONSTRUCTION]{.underline}**

**Section 1.01 --- Definitions**

As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:

\"**Asset Disposition**\" means any sale, transfer, lease, license, exchange, or other disposition (including by way of merger or consolidation) of assets of the Borrower or any other loan party, whether in a single transaction or in a series of related transactions, other than (a) sales of inventory in the ordinary course of business and (b) dispositions of worn-out, obsolete, or surplus equipment in the ordinary course of business.

\"**Blockage Notice**\" means a written notice delivered by the First Lien Agent to the Second Lien Agent in accordance with Section 9.01 hereof, declaring the commencement of a Standstill Period following the occurrence and during the continuance of a Second Lien Default, which notice shall specify in reasonable detail the nature of the Second Lien Default giving rise thereto.

\"**Borrower**\" means Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc., a Delaware corporation, with its principal place of business at 4200 Tryon Industrial Parkway, Charlotte, North Carolina 28217, and its registered agent at c/o National Registered Agents, Inc., 1209 Orange Street, Wilmington, Delaware 19801 (EIN: 84-3927561), together with its successors and assigns.

\"**Business Day**\" means any day that is not a Saturday, Sunday, or other day on which commercial banks in New York City or Philadelphia, Pennsylvania are authorized or required by law to remain closed; *provided* that, when used in connection with a SOFR-based interest determination, the term \"Business Day\" shall also exclude any day on which the Securities Industry and Financial Markets Association recommends that the fixed income departments of its members be closed for the entire day for purposes of trading in United States government securities.

\"**Casualty Event**\" means any loss, destruction, damage to, or condemnation, seizure, or taking (whether by eminent domain, compulsory acquisition, or otherwise) of Collateral giving rise to insurance proceeds, condemnation awards, or similar compensation.

\"**Collateral**\" means all assets and property of the Borrower and the other loan parties, whether now owned or hereafter acquired, whether real, personal, or mixed, and whether tangible or intangible, that are at any time subject to a Lien securing the First Lien Obligations and/or the Second Lien Obligations, including, without limitation, all accounts, chattel paper, commercial tort claims, deposit accounts, documents, equipment, fixtures, general intangibles (including intellectual property), goods, instruments, inventory, investment property, letter-of-credit rights, letters of credit, money, and all proceeds and products of any of the foregoing.

\"**DIP Financing**\" means any debtor-in-possession financing or use of cash collateral (as defined in Section 363(a) of the Bankruptcy Code) approved by the bankruptcy court in an Insolvency Proceeding involving the Borrower or any other loan party.

\"**Discharge of First Lien Obligations**\" means the date on which (a) the aggregate outstanding First Lien Obligations (other than contingent indemnification obligations and expense reimbursement obligations not yet due and payable and for which no claim has been asserted) have been indefeasibly paid in full in cash, (b) all commitments under the First Lien Credit Agreement (including all unfunded commitments under the First Lien Revolver) have been permanently terminated, and (c) all letters of credit issued under the First Lien Credit Agreement have been terminated, expired, or been cash collateralized in an amount equal to one hundred five percent (105%) of the undrawn face amount thereof.

\"**First Lien Agent**\" means Cartwright National Bank, N.A., a national banking association headquartered at 1600 Arch Street, 30th Floor, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania 19103, in its capacity as administrative agent and collateral agent under the First Lien Credit Agreement, together with its successors and assigns in such capacity.

\"**First Lien Credit Agreement**\" has the meaning set forth in the Recitals.

\"**First Lien Lenders**\" means the lenders from time to time party to the First Lien Credit Agreement, which as of the date hereof consist of a syndicate of seven (7) lenders led by the First Lien Agent, together with their respective successors and assigns.

\"**First Lien Loan Documents**\" means the First Lien Credit Agreement and all other agreements, instruments, and documents executed and delivered in connection therewith, including, without limitation, all security agreements, pledge agreements, mortgages, deeds of trust, guaranties, and other collateral documents.

\"**First Lien Maturity Date**\" means June 15, 2030.

\"**First Lien Obligations**\" means all obligations of the Borrower and the other loan parties under the First Lien Credit Agreement and the other First Lien Loan Documents, whether now existing or hereafter arising, including, without limitation, all obligations in respect of principal, interest (including interest accruing at the rate specified in the applicable First Lien Loan Document after the commencement of any Insolvency Proceeding, whether or not such interest is an allowed claim in such proceeding), premium (if any), fees, costs, charges, expenses, reimbursement obligations, indemnities, and all other amounts payable by the Borrower or any other loan party under or in respect of the First Lien Loan Documents, including all amounts payable in respect of the First Lien Term Loan A and the First Lien Revolver.

\"**First Lien Revolver**\" has the meaning set forth in the Recitals.

\"**First Lien Term Loan A**\" has the meaning set forth in the Recitals.

\"**Insolvency Proceeding**\" means any case, action, or proceeding before any court or other governmental authority relating to bankruptcy, reorganization, insolvency, liquidation, receivership, dissolution, winding-up, or relief of debtors, including (a) any case under Title 11 of the United States Code (the \"**Bankruptcy Code**\"), (b) any proceeding under the law of any jurisdiction (domestic or foreign) that is analogous to a proceeding under the Bankruptcy Code, and (c) any assignment for the benefit of creditors, composition, marshaling of assets for creditors, or other similar arrangement in respect of the debtor\'s creditors generally or any substantial portion of the debtor\'s creditors, in each case whether voluntary or involuntary.

\"**Lien**\" means any mortgage, pledge, hypothecation, assignment, deposit arrangement, encumbrance, lien (statutory or other), charge, preference, priority, or other security interest or preferential arrangement of any kind or nature whatsoever (including any conditional sale or other title retention agreement, any easement, right of way, or other encumbrance on title to real property, and any financing lease having substantially the same economic effect as any of the foregoing).

\"**Net Cash Proceeds**\" means the aggregate cash proceeds received by the Borrower or any loan party in respect of any Asset Disposition or Casualty Event, net of (a) all reasonable and customary direct costs, fees, and expenses incurred in connection with such Asset Disposition or Casualty Event (including, without limitation, brokerage commissions, finder\'s fees, legal fees, accountants\' fees, investment banking fees, survey costs, title insurance premiums, transfer taxes, and similar costs and expenses), (b) all taxes paid or reasonably estimated to be payable as a result thereof (after taking into account any available tax credits or deductions and any tax sharing arrangements), and (c) all amounts required to be applied to the repayment of indebtedness secured by a Lien on the asset or assets that are the subject of such Asset Disposition or Casualty Event that is senior to or *pari passu* with the First Lien Obligations (other than indebtedness secured by a Lien that is subject to this Agreement).

\"**Permitted Second Lien Refinancing Indebtedness**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 2.03(c).

\"**Person**\" means any natural person, corporation, limited liability company, trust, joint venture, association, company, partnership, governmental authority, or other entity.

\"**Purchase Notice**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 5.02.

\"**Purchase Price**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 5.01.

\"**Second Lien Agent**\" means Ridgeline Capital Partners, LP, a Delaware limited partnership with offices at 590 Madison Avenue, 22nd Floor, New York, New York 10022, in its capacity as administrative agent and collateral agent under the Second Lien Credit Agreement, together with its successors and assigns in such capacity.

\"**Second Lien Credit Agreement**\" has the meaning set forth in the Recitals.

\"**Second Lien Default**\" means any \"Event of Default\" as defined in, and under, the Second Lien Credit Agreement.

\"**Second Lien Lenders**\" means the lenders from time to time party to the Second Lien Credit Agreement, which as of the date hereof consist of three (3) participating lenders led by the Second Lien Agent, together with their respective successors and assigns.

\"**Second Lien Loan Documents**\" means the Second Lien Credit Agreement and all other agreements, instruments, and documents executed and delivered in connection therewith, including, without limitation, all security agreements, pledge agreements, mortgages, deeds of trust, guaranties, and other collateral documents.

\"**Second Lien Maturity Date**\" means June 15, 2031.

\"**Second Lien Obligations**\" means all obligations of the Borrower and the other loan parties under the Second Lien Credit Agreement and the other Second Lien Loan Documents, whether now existing or hereafter arising, including, without limitation, all obligations in respect of principal, interest (including interest accruing at the rate specified in the applicable Second Lien Loan Document after the commencement of any Insolvency Proceeding, whether or not such interest is an allowed claim in such proceeding), premium (if any), fees, costs, charges, expenses, reimbursement obligations, indemnities, and all other amounts payable by the Borrower or any other loan party under or in respect of the Second Lien Loan Documents, including all amounts payable in respect of the Second Lien Term Loan B.

\"**Second Lien Purchase Right**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 5.01.

\"**Second Lien Term Loan B**\" has the meaning set forth in the Recitals.

\"**SOFR**\" means the Secured Overnight Financing Rate published by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York (or a successor administrator). For purposes of illustrative calculations contained herein, SOFR is assumed to be 5.33% per annum as of the date hereof.

\"**Standstill Period**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 3.01(b).

\"**UCC**\" means the Uniform Commercial Code as in effect from time to time in the State of New York; *provided* that if, by reason of mandatory provisions of law, the perfection, the effect of perfection or non-perfection, or the priority of a Lien on any item or items of Collateral is governed by the Uniform Commercial Code as in effect in a jurisdiction other than the State of New York, then \"UCC\" shall mean the Uniform Commercial Code as in effect from time to time in such other jurisdiction for purposes of the provisions hereof relating to such perfection, effect of perfection or non-perfection, or priority.

**Section 1.02 --- Rules of Construction**

\(a\) Terms defined in the UCC (as in effect in the State of New York) that are used but not otherwise defined herein shall have the respective meanings given to such terms therein.

\(b\) The definitions of terms herein shall apply equally to the singular and plural forms of the terms defined. Whenever the context may require, any pronoun shall include the corresponding masculine, feminine, and neuter forms. The words \"include,\" \"includes,\" and \"including\" shall be deemed to be followed by the phrase \"without limitation.\" The word \"will\" shall be construed to have the same meaning and effect as the word \"shall.\" Unless the context requires otherwise, (i) any definition of or reference to any agreement, instrument, or other document herein shall be construed as referring to such agreement, instrument, or other document as from time to time amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified (subject to any restrictions on such amendments, restatements, supplements, or modifications set forth herein), (ii) any reference herein to any Person shall be construed to include such Person\'s successors and assigns, (iii) the words \"herein,\" \"hereof,\" and \"hereunder,\" and words of similar import, shall be construed to refer to this Agreement in its entirety and not to any particular provision hereof, and (iv) all references herein to Articles, Sections, Exhibits, and Schedules shall be construed to refer to Articles and Sections of, and Exhibits and Schedules to, this Agreement.

\(c\) The headings and captions used in this Agreement are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the construction or interpretation of this Agreement.

\(d\) This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, as more particularly set forth in Section 9.02.

\(e\) In the computation of periods of time from a specified date to a later specified date, the word \"from\" means \"from and including\" and the words \"to\" and \"until\" each mean \"to but excluding\"; *provided* that with respect to the computation of fees, interest, and other charges, the word \"from\" means \"from and including\" and the word \"to\" means \"to and including.\"

**[ARTICLE II --- PRIORITIES AND SUBORDINATION]{.underline}**

**Section 2.01 --- Lien Priority**

\(a\) Notwithstanding the date, time, method, manner, or order of grant, attachment, filing, recording, or perfection of any Liens securing the First Lien Obligations or the Second Lien Obligations, and notwithstanding any provision of the UCC or any other applicable law, or any deficiency or infirmity in, or any failure to perfect, any such Lien, or any other circumstance whatsoever (whether or not any such Lien is a purchase money security interest or its equivalent), the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent hereby agree that:

> \(i\) all Liens on the Collateral securing the First Lien Obligations, whether now existing or hereafter created, shall in all respects be senior and prior to all Liens on the Collateral securing the Second Lien Obligations; and
>
> \(ii\) all Liens on the Collateral securing the Second Lien Obligations, whether now existing or hereafter created, shall in all respects be junior and subordinate to all Liens on the Collateral securing the First Lien Obligations.

\(b\) The priorities established by this Section 2.01 shall not be altered or otherwise affected by (i) any amendment, modification, supplement, extension, renewal, restatement, refunding, replacement, or refinancing of the First Lien Obligations or the Second Lien Obligations, (ii) the taking or failure to take any action to perfect any Lien securing the First Lien Obligations or the Second Lien Obligations, (iii) the avoidance, invalidation, or subordination by any third party or court of competent jurisdiction of any Lien securing the First Lien Obligations or the Second Lien Obligations, or (iv) the release of any Collateral from, or the failure to attach to any Collateral of, any Lien securing the First Lien Obligations.

\(c\) The First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent each acknowledge and agree that the priorities set forth in this Section 2.01 are established by this Agreement and are not dependent on the time of creation, attachment, perfection, or recording of any Liens, or the order of filing of any financing statements under the UCC or any other applicable law. Regardless of the manner of acquisition or perfection, and regardless of whether the relevant Lien is a purchase money security interest or its equivalent, the priorities set forth in this Agreement shall govern.

**Section 2.02 --- Subordination of Second Lien**

\(a\) The Second Lien Agent, on behalf of itself and the Second Lien Lenders, hereby agrees that all Second Lien Obligations and all Liens on the Collateral securing the Second Lien Obligations are and shall be expressly subordinate and junior in right of Lien and priority to all First Lien Obligations and all Liens on the Collateral securing the First Lien Obligations, and the Second Lien Agent, on behalf of itself and the Second Lien Lenders, hereby subordinates all Liens on the Collateral securing the Second Lien Obligations to all Liens on the Collateral securing the First Lien Obligations.

\(b\) The Second Lien Agent, on behalf of itself and the Second Lien Lenders, agrees that it shall not (and shall not direct, authorize, or instruct the Second Lien Lenders to) at any time:

> \(i\) contest, challenge, object to, or otherwise dispute the priority, validity, perfection, or enforceability of any First Lien Obligations or any Lien on the Collateral securing the First Lien Obligations;
>
> \(ii\) take any action that is inconsistent with the subordination and priorities established by this Agreement;
>
> \(iii\) assert or enforce any right of marshaling with respect to the Collateral as against the First Lien Agent or the First Lien Lenders;
>
> \(iv\) assert or enforce any claim under Section 506(c) of the Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable provision of any other applicable law) as against the First Lien Agent, the First Lien Lenders, or the Collateral securing the First Lien Obligations; or
>
> \(v\) seek to have any court declare that the Second Lien Obligations or the Liens securing the Second Lien Obligations are of equal or senior priority to the First Lien Obligations or the Liens securing the First Lien Obligations.

\(c\) The subordination and agreements set forth in this Section 2.02 are for the benefit of the First Lien Agent and the First Lien Lenders and shall not be affected by any act or failure to act by the Borrower, any other loan party, or any other Person.

**Section 2.03 --- Permitted Actions by Second Lien Secured Parties**

**Section 2.03(a) --- Filing of Proofs of Claim.** Subject to the limitations set forth in this Agreement, the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Lenders may file proofs of claim, vote on any plan of reorganization or liquidation, and take all other actions necessary or appropriate to establish, assert, and preserve their claims against the Borrower and any other loan party in any Insolvency Proceeding. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such action shall be taken by the Second Lien Agent or any Second Lien Lender that is inconsistent with the subordination and priorities established by this Agreement, including the provisions of Article III (Enforcement Actions; Standstill), Article IV (Payments; Application of Proceeds), and Article VI (Insolvency Proceedings).

**Section 2.03(b) --- Permitted Amendments to Second Lien Documents.** The Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Lenders may amend, supplement, restate, or otherwise modify the Second Lien Loan Documents, and may enter into any additional Second Lien Loan Documents, in each case without the consent of the First Lien Agent or the First Lien Lenders; *provided* that no such amendment, supplement, restatement, modification, or additional document shall (a) contravene or be inconsistent with any of the terms, conditions, or provisions of this Agreement, (b) increase the aggregate principal amount of the Second Lien Obligations (except in connection with Permitted Second Lien Refinancing Indebtedness as set forth in Section 2.03(c)), (c) shorten the maturity date of the Second Lien Obligations to a date earlier than the Second Lien Maturity Date, or (d) add or modify any covenant, event of default, or other term in a manner that would materially and adversely affect the rights of the First Lien Agent or the First Lien Lenders under this Agreement.

**Section 2.03(c) --- Permitted Second Lien Refinancing Indebtedness.**

**\[BRACKETED --- UNDER NEGOTIATION\]**

The parties have not yet reached agreement on the definition and terms governing Permitted Second Lien Refinancing Indebtedness. Set forth below are the respective proposed formulations of each of the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent, followed by a drafting note identifying the outstanding disputes.

**First Lien Agent\'s Proposed Language:**

> \"**Permitted Second Lien Refinancing Indebtedness**\" means indebtedness incurred to refinance, replace, or substitute the Second Lien Obligations, *provided* that: (i) the aggregate principal amount of such refinancing indebtedness shall not exceed the aggregate principal amount of the Second Lien Obligations being refinanced (for the avoidance of doubt, no increase in principal shall be permitted); (ii) the stated final maturity date of such refinancing indebtedness shall be no earlier than the date that is ninety-one (91) days after the First Lien Maturity Date (*i.e.*, no earlier than September 14, 2030); (iii) the terms and conditions of such refinancing indebtedness (taken as a whole) shall not be more restrictive in any material respect to the Borrower than the terms and conditions of the Second Lien Obligations being refinanced; and (iv) the holders of such refinancing indebtedness shall have become party to this Agreement (or a replacement intercreditor agreement on substantially similar terms) by executing and delivering a joinder agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the First Lien Agent.

**Second Lien Agent\'s Proposed Language:**

> \"**Permitted Second Lien Refinancing Indebtedness**\" means indebtedness incurred to refinance, replace, or substitute the Second Lien Obligations, *provided* that: (i) the aggregate principal amount of such refinancing indebtedness shall not exceed one hundred twenty percent (120%) of the aggregate principal amount of the Second Lien Obligations being refinanced (*i.e.*, based on the current \$150,000,000 Second Lien Term Loan B, a maximum of \$180,000,000, reflecting a \$30,000,000 permitted increase); (ii) the stated final maturity date of such refinancing indebtedness shall be no earlier than the Second Lien Maturity Date (*i.e.*, no earlier than June 15, 2031); and (iii) the holders of such refinancing indebtedness shall have become party to this Agreement (or a replacement intercreditor agreement on substantially similar terms) by executing and delivering a joinder agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent.

**Drafting Note:** Both proposals include a joinder requirement, which is agreed in substance. The outstanding disputes are as follows: **(A) Principal Cap** --- The First Lien Agent proposes a zero-percent increase (*i.e.*, no increase above the principal amount being refinanced), while the Second Lien Agent proposes a twenty-percent (20%) increase (a \$30,000,000 permitted increase based on the current \$150,000,000 Second Lien Term Loan B). **(B) Maturity Floor** --- The First Lien Agent proposes a maturity floor of ninety-one (91) days after the First Lien Maturity Date (*i.e.*, September 14, 2030), while the Second Lien Agent proposes a maturity floor of the original Second Lien Maturity Date (*i.e.*, June 15, 2031, which already exceeds September 14, 2030). **(C) Restrictiveness Test** --- The First Lien Agent proposes a blanket \"no more restrictive in any material respect\" test applicable to the terms and conditions of the refinancing indebtedness, while the Second Lien Agent\'s proposal does not include any such restrictiveness test. The memorandum from Kesswick, Tanaka & Moreau LLP proposes compromise language to resolve each of these three disputes.

**[ARTICLE III --- ENFORCEMENT ACTIONS; STANDSTILL]{.underline}**

**Section 3.01 --- Standstill**

**Section 3.01(a) --- General Restriction on Second Lien Enforcement.** Until the Discharge of First Lien Obligations, the Second Lien Agent shall not, and shall not direct, authorize, or instruct the Second Lien Lenders to, exercise or seek to exercise any rights or remedies (including set-off or recoupment) with respect to the Collateral, or take or seek to take any action to enforce, collect, or realize upon any Collateral or any portion thereof, or commence or join with any creditor in commencing any enforcement, collection, or other remedial proceeding (judicial or otherwise) with respect to the Collateral, or commence or join in any Insolvency Proceeding against the Borrower or any other loan party, except as expressly permitted by this Agreement. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Second Lien Agent shall not take any of the following actions prior to the Discharge of First Lien Obligations (except as expressly permitted herein):

> \(i\) foreclose upon, levy against, or otherwise seek to realize upon any Collateral;
>
> \(ii\) take possession of, or otherwise exercise dominion and control over, any Collateral;
>
> \(iii\) commence, continue, or participate in any action to collect payment directly from, or enforce against, the Collateral;
>
> \(iv\) exercise any rights under any lockbox, deposit account control agreement, securities account control agreement, or similar arrangement with respect to the Collateral;
>
> \(v\) exercise any remedies of a secured creditor under the UCC or under any other applicable law with respect to the Collateral; or
>
> \(vi\) exercise any right of setoff, counterclaim, or recoupment against any Collateral.

The foregoing restrictions shall not apply (x) following the expiration or early termination of the Standstill Period in accordance with Section 3.01(b), subject to and in accordance with Section 3.03 and Section 5.01, or (y) after the Discharge of First Lien Obligations.

**Section 3.01(b) --- Standstill Period.**

**\[BRACKETED --- UNDER NEGOTIATION\]**

The parties have not yet reached agreement on the duration of, or the conditions governing early termination of, the Standstill Period. Set forth below are the respective proposed formulations of each of the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent, followed by a drafting note identifying the outstanding disputes.

**First Lien Agent\'s Proposed Language:**

> Following the occurrence and during the continuance of a Second Lien Default, the Second Lien Agent shall not exercise any rights or remedies with respect to the Collateral for a period of two hundred seventy (270) days following delivery by the First Lien Agent to the Second Lien Agent of a written notice (a \"**Blockage Notice**\") specifying the Second Lien Default giving rise thereto (the \"**Standstill Period**\"). No early termination of the Standstill Period shall be permitted under any circumstance, and the Standstill Period shall remain in effect for the full two hundred seventy (270)-day period regardless of any intervening event or action. There shall be no limit on the number of Blockage Notices that the First Lien Agent may deliver during the term of the Second Lien Credit Agreement, and each Blockage Notice shall give rise to a separate Standstill Period.

**Second Lien Agent\'s Proposed Language:**

> Following the occurrence and during the continuance of a Second Lien Default, the Second Lien Agent shall not exercise any rights or remedies with respect to the Collateral for a period of ninety (90) days following delivery by the First Lien Agent to the Second Lien Agent of a Blockage Notice specifying the Second Lien Default giving rise thereto (the \"**Standstill Period**\"). The Standstill Period shall terminate prior to the expiration of such ninety (90)-day period upon the occurrence of any of the following early termination events: (A) the First Lien Agent shall have commenced an enforcement action (including foreclosure, levy, or other realization) against any material portion of the Collateral; or (B) the filing of an Insolvency Proceeding by or against the Borrower or any material loan party. There shall be no limit on the number of Blockage Notices that the First Lien Agent may deliver during the term of the Second Lien Credit Agreement.

**Drafting Note:** Neither proposal includes a cap on the number of Blockage Notices that may be issued during the term of the Second Lien Credit Agreement. The outstanding disputes are as follows: **(A) Standstill Duration** --- The First Lien Agent proposes a two hundred seventy (270)-day standstill period, while the Second Lien Agent proposes a ninety (90)-day standstill period. **(B) Early Termination** --- The First Lien Agent proposes no early termination triggers under any circumstances, while the Second Lien Agent proposes two early termination events (*i.e.*, commencement of enforcement action by the First Lien Agent against material Collateral, or filing of an Insolvency Proceeding involving the Borrower). **(C) Blockage Notice Cap** --- Neither proposal includes a limit on the number of Blockage Notices that may be issued, but the memorandum from Kesswick, Tanaka & Moreau LLP proposes compromise language addressing the standstill duration, early termination triggers, and the introduction of a Blockage Notice cap.

**Section 3.02 --- Permitted Actions During Standstill**

Notwithstanding Section 3.01(a) and Section 3.01(b), during the pendency of a Standstill Period, the Second Lien Agent may, without being deemed to have violated the standstill obligations set forth herein, take any of the following actions:

> \(a\) **Acceleration.** The Second Lien Agent may declare all or any portion of the Second Lien Obligations to be immediately due and payable and exercise any other rights or remedies under the Second Lien Loan Documents that do not involve the exercise of remedies against the Collateral, including the right to charge default-rate interest in accordance with the Second Lien Credit Agreement;
>
> \(b\) **Proofs of Claim.** The Second Lien Agent may file proofs of claim, make filings, and take other actions necessary or appropriate to assert, preserve, or protect its claims or the claims of the Second Lien Lenders in any Insolvency Proceeding, subject to and in accordance with the limitations set forth in Article VI;
>
> \(c\) **Preservation Actions.** The Second Lien Agent may take any actions necessary to preserve the validity, perfection, and enforceability of the Second Lien liens on the Collateral, including the filing of UCC continuation statements, the maintenance of insurance, and the recording of mortgages, deeds of trust, or other documents, *provided* that no such action shall adversely affect the priority of the Liens securing the First Lien Obligations;
>
> \(d\) **Second Lien Purchase Right.** The Second Lien Agent may exercise the Second Lien Purchase Right pursuant to Section 5.01 hereof; and
>
> \(e\) **Notices and Communications.** The Second Lien Agent may deliver any notices or communications to the Borrower, any other loan party, or the First Lien Agent as required or permitted by the Second Lien Loan Documents or this Agreement.

**Section 3.03 --- Actions Following Expiration of Standstill**

Following the expiration or early termination of the Standstill Period (as determined in accordance with Section 3.01(b)), and subject to the prior exercise of the Second Lien Purchase Right pursuant to Section 5.01 (if applicable), the Second Lien Agent may exercise any and all rights and remedies available to it under the Second Lien Loan Documents and applicable law with respect to the Collateral, including the right to foreclose upon, levy against, take possession of, and otherwise realize upon the Collateral; *provided* that all proceeds of Collateral received in connection with any such exercise of rights or remedies shall be applied in accordance with Section 4.01, and the priority and subordination provisions of this Agreement shall continue to apply in full force and effect to any such exercise of rights or remedies and to the application of any proceeds thereof. For the avoidance of doubt, the Second Lien Agent\'s right to exercise remedies following the expiration or early termination of the Standstill Period shall not be deemed to alter or otherwise affect the subordination of the Second Lien Obligations and the Liens securing the Second Lien Obligations to the First Lien Obligations and the Liens securing the First Lien Obligations.

**[ARTICLE IV --- PAYMENTS; APPLICATION OF PROCEEDS]{.underline}**

**Section 4.01 --- Application of Collateral Proceeds Generally**

\(a\) All proceeds of Collateral received by the First Lien Agent, the Second Lien Agent, or any of their respective Lenders in connection with any enforcement action (including any foreclosure, levy, collection, or other realization upon the Collateral), any Insolvency Proceeding, any Asset Disposition or Casualty Event to which this Section applies, or any other event giving rise to the distribution of Collateral proceeds (whether in cash, property, securities, or otherwise), shall be applied in the following order of priority:

> **First**, to the payment in full of all First Lien Obligations in accordance with the terms of the First Lien Credit Agreement and the other First Lien Loan Documents, in such order and manner as the First Lien Agent shall determine, until the Discharge of First Lien Obligations has occurred;
>
> **Second**, after the Discharge of First Lien Obligations, to the payment in full of all Second Lien Obligations in accordance with the terms of the Second Lien Credit Agreement and the other Second Lien Loan Documents, in such order and manner as the Second Lien Agent shall determine; and
>
> **Third**, after the indefeasible payment in full of all First Lien Obligations and all Second Lien Obligations, to the Borrower or to whomsoever may be lawfully entitled thereto.

\(b\) If the Second Lien Agent or any Second Lien Lender shall at any time receive any proceeds of Collateral or any payment on account of the Second Lien Obligations that should have been applied to the payment of the First Lien Obligations in accordance with the priorities set forth in Section 4.01(a), such proceeds or payment shall be received and held in trust by the Second Lien Agent or such Second Lien Lender for the benefit of the First Lien Agent and shall be promptly (and in any event within two (2) Business Days of receipt) turned over to the First Lien Agent in the same form as received (with any necessary endorsements) for application to the First Lien Obligations in accordance with the First Lien Credit Agreement. The First Lien Agent is hereby irrevocably authorized and directed to accept any such turnover payment.

\(c\) Nothing in this Section 4.01 shall be construed to prevent the Borrower from making regularly scheduled payments of principal and interest on the Second Lien Obligations in accordance with the Second Lien Credit Agreement so long as no payment default or payment blockage event has occurred under the First Lien Credit Agreement or this Agreement.

**Section 4.02 --- Application of Asset Disposition and Casualty Proceeds**

**\[BRACKETED --- UNDER NEGOTIATION\]**

The parties have not yet reached agreement on the application of Net Cash Proceeds from Asset Dispositions and Casualty Events. Set forth below are the respective proposed formulations of each of the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent, followed by a drafting note identifying the outstanding disputes.

**First Lien Agent\'s Proposed Language:**

> All Net Cash Proceeds received by the Borrower or any other loan party in respect of any Asset Disposition or Casualty Event shall be applied as follows: one hundred percent (100%) of such Net Cash Proceeds shall be applied to prepay the First Lien Obligations in accordance with the mandatory prepayment provisions of the First Lien Credit Agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, the reinvestment rights granted to the Borrower under Section \[●\] of the First Lien Credit Agreement shall be superseded by this Section 4.02, and no reinvestment in replacement assets shall be permitted with respect to any such Net Cash Proceeds. No portion of such Net Cash Proceeds shall be available for prepayment of the Second Lien Obligations until the Discharge of First Lien Obligations shall have occurred.

**Second Lien Agent\'s Proposed Language:**

> All Net Cash Proceeds received by the Borrower or any other loan party in respect of any Asset Disposition or Casualty Event shall be applied in accordance with the mandatory prepayment provisions of the First Lien Credit Agreement. The Borrower shall retain reinvestment rights in respect of such Net Cash Proceeds as set forth in the First Lien Credit Agreement (including any three hundred sixty-five (365)-day reinvestment period set forth therein). Net Cash Proceeds that are not reinvested in replacement assets within the applicable reinvestment period (or that exceed the amount required for mandatory prepayment of the First Lien Obligations under the First Lien Credit Agreement) shall be applied to prepay the Second Lien Obligations in accordance with the mandatory prepayment provisions of the Second Lien Credit Agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, the Borrower\'s reinvestment rights under the First Lien Credit Agreement shall not be impaired by this Agreement.

**Drafting Note:** The First Lien Credit Agreement contains the following asset sale prepayment provisions relevant to this Section: (a) an individual Asset Disposition threshold of \$5,000,000 (such that Asset Dispositions with Net Cash Proceeds below such amount are not subject to mandatory prepayment); (b) an annual aggregate threshold of \$15,000,000 (such that the mandatory prepayment requirement is triggered only when Net Cash Proceeds from all Asset Dispositions in a fiscal year exceed such aggregate amount); and (c) a three hundred sixty-five (365)-day reinvestment period during which the Borrower may apply Net Cash Proceeds to the acquisition of replacement assets in lieu of mandatory prepayment. The outstanding disputes include: **(A) Reinvestment Rights** --- The First Lien Agent proposes to override the reinvestment rights granted under the First Lien Credit Agreement and impose a 100% hard sweep of all Net Cash Proceeds, while the Second Lien Agent proposes to preserve the reinvestment rights under the First Lien Credit Agreement (including the 365-day reinvestment period) and permit residual proceeds to flow to the Second Lien Obligations. **(B) Anti-Evasion Language** --- Neither party\'s bracketed proposal includes anti-evasion language to prevent the Borrower from structuring Asset Dispositions below the individual \$5,000,000 threshold or the aggregate \$15,000,000 annual threshold to avoid the mandatory prepayment requirement. **(C) Reporting Mechanism** --- Neither party\'s bracketed proposal includes a mechanism for the periodic reporting and tracking of Net Cash Proceeds from Asset Dispositions and Casualty Events. The memorandum from Kesswick, Tanaka & Moreau LLP proposes compromise language introducing a bifurcated approach with anti-evasion protections and a quarterly reporting mechanism.

**[ARTICLE V --- PURCHASE OPTION]{.underline}**

**Section 5.01 --- Second Lien Purchase Right**

**\[BRACKETED --- UNDER NEGOTIATION\]**

The parties have not yet reached agreement on the terms of the Second Lien Purchase Right. Set forth below are the respective proposed formulations of each of the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent, followed by a drafting note identifying the outstanding disputes.

**First Lien Agent\'s Proposed Language:**

> At any time following the occurrence and during the continuance of an Event of Default under the First Lien Credit Agreement, the Second Lien Agent (on behalf of the Second Lien Lenders) shall have the right (but not the obligation) to purchase all (but not less than all) of the First Lien Obligations (the \"**Second Lien Purchase Right**\") by paying to the First Lien Agent in immediately available funds a purchase price (the \"**Purchase Price**\") equal to the sum of: (i) one hundred percent (100%) of the aggregate outstanding principal amount of all funded First Lien Obligations (including all amounts outstanding under the First Lien Term Loan A and all drawn amounts under the First Lien Revolver); *plus* (ii) all accrued and unpaid interest thereon, calculated at the default rate applicable under the First Lien Credit Agreement, through and including the date of purchase; *plus* (iii) all fees, costs, expenses, indemnities, breakage costs, and other amounts then due and payable under the First Lien Loan Documents, whether or not invoiced. In addition, as a condition to the purchase, the Second Lien Agent (or its designee) shall assume all unfunded commitments under the First Lien Revolver (which, as of the date hereof, total One Hundred Twenty-Five Million Dollars (\$125,000,000) in aggregate commitments, less any amounts drawn thereunder as of the date of purchase) by executing and delivering to the First Lien Agent an assumption agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the First Lien Agent. The purchase shall be on a par, non-recourse, as-is, where-is basis, without representation or warranty of any kind by the First Lien Agent or any First Lien Lender (other than representations as to the authority of the assigning lender to assign and the enforceability of the transfer).

**Second Lien Agent\'s Proposed Language:**

> At any time following the occurrence and during the continuance of an Event of Default under the First Lien Credit Agreement or the Second Lien Credit Agreement (or, if earlier, upon delivery by the First Lien Agent of a notice of acceleration of the First Lien Obligations), the Second Lien Agent (on behalf of the Second Lien Lenders) shall have the right (but not the obligation) to purchase all (but not less than all) of the First Lien Obligations (the \"**Second Lien Purchase Right**\") by paying to the First Lien Agent in immediately available funds a purchase price (the \"**Purchase Price**\") equal to the lesser of:
>
> \(A\) the sum of: (i) one hundred percent (100%) of the aggregate outstanding principal amount of all funded First Lien Obligations (including all amounts outstanding under the First Lien Term Loan A and all drawn amounts under the First Lien Revolver); *plus* (ii) all accrued and unpaid interest thereon, calculated at the non-default contract rate applicable under the First Lien Credit Agreement, through and including the date of purchase; *plus* (iii) all fees, costs, expenses, indemnities, and other amounts then due and payable under the First Lien Loan Documents; and
>
> \(B\) the fair market value of the First Lien Obligations as determined by a dealer poll of no fewer than three (3) independent, nationally recognized dealers in distressed debt conducted by a mutually acceptable third-party agent, with such dealer poll to be completed no more than ten (10) Business Days following delivery of a Purchase Notice.
>
> No obligation to assume unfunded commitments under the First Lien Revolver shall be imposed as a condition to the exercise of the Second Lien Purchase Right, and the First Lien Revolver commitments shall terminate upon consummation of the purchase.

**Drafting Note:** The disputes on this Section are as follows: **(A) Purchase Price** --- The First Lien Agent proposes a par purchase at 100% of principal plus default-rate accrued interest plus fees, while the Second Lien Agent proposes the lesser of par (at non-default contract-rate accrued interest) or fair market value as determined by a dealer poll. **(B) Interest Rate for Accrued Interest** --- The First Lien Agent proposes that accrued interest be calculated at the default rate applicable under the First Lien Credit Agreement, while the Second Lien Agent proposes that accrued interest be calculated at the non-default contract rate. **(C) Mandatory vs. Optional Assumption of Unfunded Revolver Commitments** --- The First Lien Agent proposes that, as a condition to the purchase, the Second Lien Agent (or its designee) must assume all unfunded commitments under the First Lien Revolver (\$125,000,000 in aggregate), while the Second Lien Agent proposes that no assumption of unfunded revolver commitments shall be required and that such commitments shall terminate upon consummation of the purchase. **(D) Scope of Exercise Triggers** --- The First Lien Agent proposes that the Second Lien Purchase Right may be exercised only upon the occurrence and during the continuance of an Event of Default under the First Lien Credit Agreement, while the Second Lien Agent proposes broader exercise triggers, including an Event of Default under the First Lien Credit Agreement, an Event of Default under the Second Lien Credit Agreement, or the delivery by the First Lien Agent of a notice of acceleration of the First Lien Obligations. The memorandum from Kesswick, Tanaka & Moreau LLP proposes compromise language addressing each of these four disputes.

**Section 5.02 --- Mechanics of Purchase**

\(a\) The Second Lien Agent shall exercise the Second Lien Purchase Right by delivering to the First Lien Agent a written notice (a \"**Purchase Notice**\") specifying (i) that the Second Lien Agent is exercising the Second Lien Purchase Right pursuant to Section 5.01 hereof, (ii) the proposed purchase date (which shall be not fewer than ten (10) nor more than twenty (20) Business Days after delivery of the Purchase Notice), and (iii) the identity of the purchaser (if other than the Second Lien Agent). The Purchase Notice, once delivered, shall be irrevocable.

\(b\) On the proposed purchase date specified in the Purchase Notice, the closing of the purchase shall occur at the offices of the First Lien Agent (or such other location as the parties may agree). At the closing:

> \(i\) the First Lien Agent shall deliver (or cause to be delivered) to the Second Lien Agent (or its designee) duly executed assignment agreements (on standard Loan Syndications and Trading Association forms, or such other forms as may be reasonably satisfactory to the parties), assigning to the Second Lien Agent (or its designee), without recourse, representation, or warranty of any kind (other than customary representations as to the authority of the assigning lender to assign and the enforceability of the assignment), all of the First Lien Obligations, together with all First Lien Loan Documents; and
>
> \(ii\) the Second Lien Agent (or its designee) shall pay to the First Lien Agent, in immediately available funds by wire transfer to an account specified by the First Lien Agent, the Purchase Price as calculated in accordance with Section 5.01.

\(c\) All costs and expenses of the purchase (including, without limitation, transfer taxes, recording fees, and reasonable attorneys\' fees) shall be borne by the Second Lien Agent (or its designee).

\(d\) Prior to the closing, the First Lien Agent shall deliver to the Second Lien Agent a payoff letter setting forth in reasonable detail the calculation of the Purchase Price, including all components thereof. The payoff letter shall be delivered no later than three (3) Business Days prior to the proposed purchase date.

**Section 5.03 --- Effect of Purchase**

\(a\) Upon consummation of the purchase contemplated by Section 5.01 and payment of the Purchase Price in full, (i) the First Lien Agent and the First Lien Lenders shall have no further rights, obligations, or liabilities under this Agreement or the First Lien Loan Documents (other than rights and obligations that, by their terms, survive the repayment of the First Lien Obligations), and (ii) the Second Lien Agent (or its designee) shall be deemed to be a \"First Lien Lender\" under the First Lien Credit Agreement for all purposes, with all of the rights, benefits, and obligations of a First Lien Lender thereunder.

\(b\) If, upon consummation of the purchase, the Second Lien Agent (or its designee) holds both the First Lien Obligations and the Second Lien Obligations, this Agreement shall terminate upon the written direction of such holder. If the First Lien Obligations have been assigned to a designee of the Second Lien Agent that does not also hold the Second Lien Obligations, this Agreement shall terminate upon the Discharge of First Lien Obligations (as redetermined after giving effect to the assignment).

\(c\) Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement pursuant to this Section 5.03, the provisions of Sections 8.02, 9.02, 9.03, and 9.07 shall survive such termination.

**[ARTICLE VI --- INSOLVENCY PROCEEDINGS]{.underline}**

**Section 6.01 --- DIP Financing**

\(a\) If, in any Insolvency Proceeding, the Borrower (or any trustee, examiner, or debtor-in-possession acting on behalf of the Borrower) desires to obtain DIP Financing, the Second Lien Agent agrees that it shall not object to, contest, oppose, or impede the entry of any order approving such DIP Financing, or any use of cash collateral that constitutes Collateral, if the First Lien Agent has consented to (or has not objected to) such DIP Financing or use of cash collateral; *provided* that:

> \(i\) the Second Lien Agent shall retain its Lien on the Collateral (including any proceeds thereof) subject to the priority of the Liens securing the First Lien Obligations and the Liens securing any DIP Financing, which DIP Financing Liens may be senior in priority to the Liens securing both the First Lien Obligations and the Second Lien Obligations or may be *pari passu* with the Liens securing the First Lien Obligations but in all events senior to the Liens securing the Second Lien Obligations;
>
> \(ii\) the Second Lien Agent shall receive adequate protection of its interests in the Collateral in the form of (A) replacement Liens on the Collateral (including any after-acquired property), which replacement Liens shall be junior and subordinate to (1) the Liens securing the First Lien Obligations, (2) the Liens securing any DIP Financing, and (3) any adequate protection Liens granted to the First Lien Agent, and (B) superpriority administrative expense claims under Section 507(b) of the Bankruptcy Code, which superpriority claims shall be junior and subordinate to the superpriority administrative expense claims (if any) of the First Lien Agent and the lenders providing DIP Financing; and
>
> \(iii\) the DIP Financing order shall not compel the Borrower to seek confirmation of a specific plan of reorganization for which all or substantially all of the material terms have been proposed or approved by the First Lien Agent without the consent of the Second Lien Agent (which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed).

\(b\) The Second Lien Agent further agrees that it shall not propose or support any DIP Financing that is not consented to by the First Lien Agent, and shall not seek or accept DIP Financing liens or superpriority claims that are senior to or *pari passu* with the Liens or claims of the First Lien Agent with respect to the Collateral.

**Section 6.02 --- Relief from the Automatic Stay**

The Second Lien Agent agrees that it shall not seek, and shall not direct, authorize, or instruct the Second Lien Lenders to seek, relief from the automatic stay imposed by Section 362 of the Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable provision of any other applicable law) with respect to any Collateral, without the prior written consent of the First Lien Agent; *provided* that the foregoing restriction shall not apply (a) after the Discharge of First Lien Obligations, or (b) if the First Lien Agent has sought and obtained relief from the automatic stay with respect to the same Collateral. For the avoidance of doubt, the Second Lien Agent may seek relief from the automatic stay with respect to Collateral at any time after the Discharge of First Lien Obligations.

**Section 6.03 --- Adequate Protection**

If the First Lien Agent is granted adequate protection in any Insolvency Proceeding (whether in the form of cash payments, additional or replacement Liens, superpriority claims, or otherwise), the Second Lien Agent agrees that it shall not seek or accept adequate protection for its Second Lien Liens on the Collateral in the form of:

> \(a\) cash payments, except to the extent that such cash payments are made out of the assets of the estate and are not made from proceeds of Collateral that would otherwise be distributable to the First Lien Agent;
>
> \(b\) additional or replacement Liens on the Collateral, except for replacement Liens that are in all respects junior and subordinate to (i) the Liens securing the First Lien Obligations, (ii) the Liens securing any DIP Financing consented to by the First Lien Agent, and (iii) the adequate protection Liens (if any) granted to the First Lien Agent; or
>
> \(c\) superpriority administrative expense claims, except for superpriority claims that are in all respects junior and subordinate to the superpriority administrative expense claims (if any) of the First Lien Agent and the lenders providing DIP Financing.

Nothing in this Section 6.03 shall be deemed to limit the Second Lien Agent\'s right to seek adequate protection in the form of a reservation of rights to assert claims against assets of the Borrower that do not constitute Collateral.

**Section 6.04 --- Post-Petition Interest**

The Second Lien Agent agrees that, to the extent the First Lien Agent is entitled to receive, or seeks to receive, post-petition interest on the First Lien Obligations in any Insolvency Proceeding (whether pursuant to Section 506(b) of the Bankruptcy Code or otherwise), the Second Lien Agent shall not object to, contest, or oppose the allowance and payment of such post-petition interest on the First Lien Obligations. The Second Lien Agent further agrees that the First Lien Obligations shall include, for purposes of this Agreement, all post-petition interest (at the applicable contract rate or default rate, as the case may be) accruing on the First Lien Obligations after the commencement of any Insolvency Proceeding, whether or not such post-petition interest is an allowed claim in such Insolvency Proceeding and whether or not the claim of the First Lien Agent is oversecured.

**Section 6.05 --- No Challenge to First Lien Claims**

The Second Lien Agent agrees that it shall not, and shall not direct, authorize, or instruct the Second Lien Lenders to, challenge, contest, dispute, or support any other Person in challenging, contesting, or disputing, in any Insolvency Proceeding or otherwise, the amount, validity, perfection, priority, enforceability, or extent of the First Lien Obligations or the Liens on the Collateral securing the First Lien Obligations, or the rights of the First Lien Agent or the First Lien Lenders with respect thereto. This Section 6.05 shall not be construed to limit the right of the Second Lien Agent to assert, in any Insolvency Proceeding, claims or defenses that are personal to the Second Lien Agent or the Second Lien Lenders and do not relate to the First Lien Obligations or the Liens securing the First Lien Obligations.

**Section 6.06 --- Asset Sales in Insolvency**

\(a\) The Second Lien Agent agrees that it shall not object to, contest, oppose, or impede a sale, lease, or other disposition of Collateral under Section 363 of the Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable provision of any other applicable law) if the First Lien Agent has consented to such sale, lease, or disposition; *provided* that:

> \(i\) the Second Lien Agent\'s Lien shall attach to the proceeds of such sale, lease, or disposition with the same priority and subordination as set forth in this Agreement; and
>
> \(ii\) the Net Cash Proceeds of such sale, lease, or disposition shall be applied in accordance with Section 4.01.

\(b\) The Second Lien Agent shall be deemed to have consented to any sale of Collateral that is free and clear of all Liens (including the Liens of the Second Lien Agent) pursuant to Section 363(f) of the Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable provision of any other applicable law), so long as the First Lien Agent has consented to such sale and the Lien of the Second Lien Agent attaches to the proceeds of such sale as provided in Section 6.06(a)(i).

\(c\) Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Section 6.06 shall be construed to prevent the Second Lien Agent from (i) bidding for and purchasing Collateral at any sale conducted in accordance with Section 363 of the Bankruptcy Code, *provided* that the Second Lien Agent shall not credit bid the Second Lien Obligations (or any portion thereof) in any such sale unless the First Lien Obligations have been indefeasibly paid in full in cash, or (ii) objecting to the sale of Collateral to the extent that the proposed sale terms do not provide for the attachment of the Second Lien Agent\'s Lien to the proceeds of such sale.

**[ARTICLE VII --- AGREEMENTS WITH RESPECT TO THE BORROWER]{.underline}**

**Section 7.01 --- Borrower Consent**

The Borrower hereby acknowledges and consents to the terms and provisions of this Agreement, including, without limitation, the subordination, priority, standstill, payment application, purchase option, and insolvency provisions set forth herein. The Borrower agrees that it shall not take any action, or fail to take any action, that is inconsistent with the provisions of this Agreement. The Borrower further agrees that it shall comply with and be bound by all provisions of this Agreement that are applicable to the Borrower, and that the rights, remedies, and priorities established by this Agreement are for the benefit of the First Lien Agent, the First Lien Lenders, the Second Lien Agent, and the Second Lien Lenders, and may be enforced directly by any of them.

**Section 7.02 --- Notices to Borrower**

Any notice, request, consent, demand, or other communication required or permitted to be given to the Borrower under or in connection with this Agreement shall be given in accordance with Section 9.01 hereof and shall be addressed to the Borrower at the address and to the attention of the persons specified therein. The Borrower agrees to provide prompt written notice to the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent of any change in its address, facsimile number, email address, or contact persons for purposes of notices hereunder.

**Section 8.01 --- Representations of Each Party**

Each of the First Lien Agent, the Second Lien Agent, and the Borrower hereby represents and warrants to each of the other parties hereto, as of the date hereof, as follows:

The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement and the consummation of the transactions contemplated hereby have been duly authorized by all necessary corporate, partnership, or other organizational action on the part of such party.

>
> \(d\) **No Conflicts.** The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement by such party, and the consummation by such party of the transactions contemplated hereby, do not and will not (i) violate any provision of the organizational documents of such party, (ii) violate any applicable law, statute, rule, regulation, order, writ, judgment, injunction, decree, or determination applicable to such party or any of its properties, or (iii) result in a breach of, or constitute a default under, any material agreement, contract, instrument, or other obligation to which such party is a party or by which it or any of its properties is bound, except, in the case of clauses (ii) and (iii), as would not reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect on the ability of such party to perform its obligations hereunder.

**Section 8.02 --- No Other Representations**

Except as expressly set forth in Section 8.01, no party hereto makes any representation or warranty to any other party hereto regarding:

This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, and by the different parties hereto in separate counterparts, each of which when so executed shall be deemed to be an original and all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same agreement. Delivery of an executed counterpart of a signature page to this Agreement by facsimile transmission or by electronic means (including in \".pdf\" or \".tif\" format) shall be equally effective as delivery of a manually executed counterpart of this Agreement. The words \"execution,\" \"signed,\" \"signature,\" and words of like import in this Agreement shall be deemed to include electronic signatures or the keeping of records in electronic form, each of which shall be of the same legal effect, validity, or enforceability as a manually executed signature or the use of a paper-based recordkeeping system, as the case may be, to the extent and as provided for in any applicable law, including the Federal Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act, the New York State Electronic Signatures and Records Act, or any other similar state laws based on the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act.

This Agreement shall terminate upon the earliest to occur of the following: (a) the Discharge of First Lien Obligations and (b) the indefeasible payment in full in cash of all Second Lien Obligations and the permanent termination of all commitments under the Second Lien Credit Agreement; *provided* that the provisions of Sections 8.02, 9.02, 9.03, and 9.07 shall survive termination of this Agreement and shall remain in full force and effect indefinitely. Upon termination of this Agreement in accordance with this Section 9.09, this Agreement shall be of no further force or effect, and the First Lien Agent, the Second Lien Agent, and the Borrower shall be released from all of their respective obligations hereunder (other than obligations under the surviving provisions specified above).

In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between the provisions of this Agreement and the provisions of the First Lien Credit Agreement, the Second Lien Credit Agreement, any First Lien Loan Document, any Second Lien Loan Document, or any other agreement, instrument, or document (in each case, with respect to the matters addressed herein), the provisions of this Agreement shall govern and control as among the First Lien Agent, the First Lien Lenders, the Second Lien Agent, the Second Lien Lenders, and the Borrower; *provided* that nothing in this Section 9.10 shall affect the rights and obligations of any party to this Agreement as against any Person that is not a party to this Agreement.
