**(d) Officer\'s Certificates.** The Administrative Agent shall have received a certificate of a Responsible Officer of the Borrower, dated as of the Closing Date, certifying that (i) the conditions set forth in Sections 3.01(i), 3.01(j), and 3.01(k) have been satisfied, (ii) attached thereto are true and correct copies of the organizational and authorization documents described in Section 3.01(c), and (iii) after giving effect to the Transactions, the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries, on a consolidated basis, are Solvent.

**(e) Security Interests; Collateral.** The Administrative Agent shall have received evidence reasonably satisfactory to it that the Security Documents create in favor of the Collateral Agent, for the benefit of the Secured Parties, a valid and perfected first-priority Lien (subject to Permitted Liens) on substantially all of the personal property Collateral of the Loan Parties required to be pledged on the Closing Date, including:

> \(i\) duly executed UCC financing statements suitable for filing in all jurisdictions reasonably deemed necessary by the Administrative Agent;
>
> \(ii\) original stock certificates or other certificated Equity Interests constituting Collateral, to the extent required by the Security Agreement, accompanied by undated stock powers or other transfer instruments endorsed in blank;
>
> \(iii\) executed control agreements with respect to deposit accounts, securities accounts, and other investment property to the extent required by the Security Agreement and reasonably obtainable on the Closing Date or within the time periods set forth in Schedule 5.11;
>
> \(iv\) searches of UCC, tax, judgment, and lien filings demonstrating the absence of Liens on the Collateral other than Permitted Liens or Liens to be released on the Closing Date; and
>
> \(v\) such payoff letters, lien releases, and termination statements as the Administrative Agent shall reasonably require in connection with the repayment of the Existing Credit Facility.

**(f) Insurance.** The Administrative Agent shall have received certificates of insurance, endorsements, or other evidence reasonably satisfactory to it that the Loan Parties maintain insurance in compliance with Section 5.05 and naming the Administrative Agent, for the benefit of the Secured Parties, as additional insured and lender loss payee, as applicable.

**(g) Fees and Expenses.** The Administrative Agent and the Lenders shall have received all fees and other amounts due and payable on or prior to the Closing Date, including all fees payable pursuant to the Fee Letter and all reasonable and documented out-of-pocket expenses of the Administrative Agent and its counsel, to the extent invoiced at least two Business Days prior to the Closing Date (except as otherwise agreed by the Borrower).

**(h) Intercreditor Agreement; Second Lien Facility.** The Administrative Agent shall have received an executed counterpart of the Intercreditor Agreement, duly executed by the Borrower, the Administrative Agent, Ridgeline Capital Partners, LP, as Second Lien Agent, and the other parties thereto, and evidence reasonably satisfactory to the Administrative Agent that the Second Lien Credit Agreement providing for a \$150,000,000 Second Lien Term Loan B shall be, or has been substantially concurrently, executed and delivered on terms consistent in all material respects with the Intercreditor Agreement and Section 6.09.

**(i) Payoff of Existing Credit Facility.** The Administrative Agent shall have received evidence reasonably satisfactory to it that immediately upon the funding of the Facilities on the Closing Date, the Existing Credit Facility maturing August 15, 2025 shall be repaid in full, all commitments thereunder shall be terminated, and all Liens securing obligations thereunder shall be released (subject to the filing of post-closing termination statements if customary and agreed).

**(j) Representations and Warranties.** The representations and warranties contained in Article IV and in each other Loan Document shall be true and correct in all material respects on and as of the Closing Date; *provided* that any such representation and warranty that is qualified by materiality, Material Adverse Effect, or similar language shall be true and correct in all respects on and as of the Closing Date; and *provided further* that any such representation and warranty expressly made as of an earlier date shall be true and correct in all material respects as of such earlier date.

**(k) No Default; No Material Adverse Effect.** No Default or Event of Default shall have occurred and be continuing or would result from the Transactions, and since the date of the most recent audited financial statements delivered to the Administrative Agent prior to the Closing Date, no Material Adverse Effect shall have occurred.

**(l) Financial Statements.** The Administrative Agent and the Lenders shall have received the historical audited consolidated financial statements of the Borrower and its Subsidiaries for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 and unaudited consolidated financial statements for the fiscal quarter ended March 31, 2025, which financial statements shall reflect LTM EBITDA of \$89,400,000 as of the most recently delivered financial statements.

**(m) Know Your Customer; USA PATRIOT Act; AML.** At least three Business Days prior to the Closing Date, the Borrower shall have delivered to the Administrative Agent and each Lender that shall have requested the same in writing at least 10 Business Days prior to the Closing Date, all documentation and other information required by bank regulatory authorities under applicable \"know your customer\" and anti-money laundering rules and regulations, including the USA PATRIOT Act and beneficial ownership regulations.

**(n) Borrowing Request.** The Administrative Agent shall have received a Borrowing Request with respect to the Loans to be funded on the Closing Date, duly executed by a Responsible Officer of the Borrower.

**[Section 3.02 --- Conditions to Each Revolving Borrowing and LC Issuance]{.underline}**

The obligation of each Revolving Lender to make any Revolving Loan after the Closing Date, and the obligation of the Issuing Bank to issue, amend, renew, or extend any Letter of Credit after the Closing Date, is subject to the satisfaction of the following conditions precedent:

**(a)** The Administrative Agent or the Issuing Bank, as applicable, shall have received a duly executed Borrowing Request, Interest Election Request, or application for issuance, amendment, renewal, or extension of a Letter of Credit, as applicable.

**(b)** The representations and warranties of the Borrower and each other Loan Party contained in Article IV and in each other Loan Document shall be true and correct in all material respects on and as of the date of such borrowing or issuance, amendment, renewal, or extension, except to the extent such representations and warranties expressly relate to an earlier date, in which case they shall be true and correct in all material respects as of such earlier date; *provided* that any representation and warranty qualified by materiality, Material Adverse Effect, or similar language shall be true and correct in all respects.

**(c)** No Default or Event of Default shall have occurred and be continuing, or would result from such borrowing or issuance, amendment, renewal, or extension.

**(d)** After giving effect to such borrowing or issuance, amendment, renewal, or extension, the Total Revolving Exposure shall not exceed the aggregate Revolving Commitments then in effect, and the aggregate LC Exposure shall not exceed the Letter of Credit Sub-Facility limit.

Each Borrowing Request and each request for issuance, amendment, renewal, or extension of a Letter of Credit shall be deemed to constitute a representation and warranty by the Borrower on the date thereof as to the matters specified in this Section 3.02.

**[ARTICLE IV --- REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES]{.underline}**

To induce the Administrative Agent, the Issuing Bank, and the Lenders to enter into this Agreement and to make the Loans and issue the Letters of Credit, the Borrower represents and warrants to the Administrative Agent, the Issuing Bank, and each Lender, on the Closing Date and on each date required by Section 3.02, that:

**[Section 4.01 --- Organization; Powers]{.underline}**

Each of the Borrower and each Guarantor (a) is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing (to the extent such concept is applicable) under the laws of its jurisdiction of organization, (b) has all requisite corporate, limited liability company, or other organizational power and authority to own or lease its properties and to carry on its business as now conducted and as proposed to be conducted, and (c) is duly qualified and in good standing as a foreign entity in each jurisdiction where the nature of its business or the ownership or lease of its properties requires such qualification, except, in the case of clauses (b) and (c), where the failure to have such power, authority, qualification, or good standing would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect. The Borrower is Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc., a corporation duly incorporated and validly existing under the laws of the State of Delaware.

**[Section 4.02 --- Authorization; No Conflicts]{.underline}**

The execution, delivery, and performance by each Loan Party of each Loan Document to which it is a party, and the consummation of the Transactions, are within such Loan Party\'s organizational powers, have been duly authorized by all necessary organizational action, and do not and will not:

**(a)** contravene the terms of any of such Loan Party\'s Organizational Documents;

**(b)** conflict with or result in any breach or contravention of, or the creation of any Lien under, or require any payment to be made under, (i) any material Indebtedness of the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary, or (ii) any order, injunction, writ, or decree of any Governmental Authority or any arbitral award to which the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary or any of their respective property is subject; or

**(c)** violate any Applicable Law;

except, in the case of clauses (b) and (c), to the extent such conflict, breach, contravention, creation of Lien, payment, or violation would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.

**[Section 4.03 --- Governmental Approvals]{.underline}**

No action, consent, approval, authorization, registration, filing, or other order of, or filing with, any Governmental Authority is required for the due execution, delivery, and performance by any Loan Party of any Loan Document to which it is a party or the consummation of the Transactions, except for (a) filings and recordings necessary to perfect Liens created under the Security Documents, (b) routine corporate and tax filings, and (c) such actions, consents, approvals, authorizations, registrations, filings, or orders the failure of which to obtain or make would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.

**[Section 4.04 --- Financial Statements; No Material Adverse Effect]{.underline}**

**(a)** The Borrower has heretofore furnished to the Administrative Agent and the Lenders (i) its audited consolidated balance sheet and related statements of income, stockholders\' equity, and cash flows for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 and (ii) its unaudited consolidated balance sheet and related statements of income and cash flows for the fiscal quarter ended March 31, 2025. Such financial statements fairly present, in all material respects, the consolidated financial condition of the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries as of the dates thereof and their consolidated results of operations and cash flows for the periods covered thereby in accordance with GAAP consistently applied, subject, in the case of unaudited financial statements, to normal year-end audit adjustments and the absence of footnotes.

**(b)** As of the most recently delivered financial statements referred to above, the Borrower had LTM EBITDA of \$89,400,000.

**(c)** Since December 31, 2024, there has been no event, change, or condition that has had, or would reasonably be expected to have, a Material Adverse Effect.

**[Section 4.05 --- Litigation]{.underline}**

Except as disclosed on Schedule 4.05, there are no actions, suits, investigations, claims, or proceedings at law or in equity or by or before any Governmental Authority or arbitrator pending or, to the knowledge of the Borrower, threatened in writing against or affecting the Borrower or any of its Restricted Subsidiaries or any of their respective properties, rights, or assets that, individually or in the aggregate, would reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.

**[Section 4.06 --- Compliance with Laws; ERISA]{.underline}**

**(a) Compliance with Laws.** The Borrower and each Restricted Subsidiary are in compliance with all Applicable Laws, including Environmental Laws, ERISA, and all laws relating to labor, health and safety, and zoning, except where the failure to do so would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.

**(b) ERISA.** Except as would not reasonably be expected to result in a Material Adverse Effect:

> \(i\) each Plan is in material compliance with the applicable provisions of ERISA, the Code, and other applicable federal or state law;
>
> \(ii\) no ERISA Event has occurred or is reasonably expected to occur;
>
> \(iii\) no \"Reportable Event\" (as defined in Section 4043(c) of ERISA, other than those events as to which the 30-day notice period is waived) has occurred within the last six years with respect to any Plan; and
>
> \(iv\) neither the Borrower nor any ERISA Affiliate has incurred, or reasonably expects to incur, any liability under Title IV of ERISA with respect to any Plan or \"**Multiemployer Plan**\" (meaning a \"multiemployer plan,\" as defined in Section 4001(a)(3) of ERISA).

**[Section 4.07 --- Taxes]{.underline}**

The Borrower and each Restricted Subsidiary have filed all federal, state, local, and foreign tax returns and reports required to be filed by them and have paid all material Taxes due and payable by them, except (a) Taxes that are being contested in good faith by appropriate proceedings and for which adequate reserves have been established in accordance with GAAP or (b) to the extent the failure to do so would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.

**[Section 4.08 --- Properties; Title to Collateral]{.underline}**

**(a)** The Borrower and each Restricted Subsidiary have good and marketable title to, or valid leasehold interests in, all of their Real Property and all other properties and assets material to the operation of their business, except where the failure to have such title or interests would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.

**(b)** The Borrower and each Loan Party has good title to, rights in, or the power to transfer each item of Collateral purported to be granted by it under the Security Documents, free and clear of any Liens except Permitted Liens.

**(c)** The Security Documents, when executed and delivered and, where applicable, filed or recorded, create valid and enforceable Liens on the Collateral purported to be covered thereby, and such Liens constitute perfected first-priority Liens (subject only to Permitted Liens) on all right, title, and interest of the Loan Parties in such Collateral, to the extent perfection can be achieved by filing UCC financing statements, possession, control, or the taking of the other actions contemplated thereby.

**[Section 4.09 --- Insurance]{.underline}**

The Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries maintain insurance with financially sound and reputable insurance companies against such risks and in such amounts as are customarily maintained by similarly situated companies engaged in the same or similar businesses and as required by Section 5.05. Such insurance includes property, casualty, liability, workers\' compensation, business interruption, and other coverage customary for the industries in which the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries operate.

**[Section 4.10 --- Solvency]{.underline}**

On the Closing Date, after giving effect to the Transactions, including the incurrence of \$475,000,000 of First Lien Obligations and \$150,000,000 of Second Lien Obligations, totaling \$625,000,000 of aggregate secured debt, the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries, on a consolidated basis, are Solvent.

**[Section 4.11 --- Disclosure]{.underline}**

No written information, report, financial statement, exhibit, or schedule furnished by or on behalf of the Borrower to the Administrative Agent or any Lender in connection with the negotiation of this Agreement or any other Loan Document or delivered hereunder or thereunder (when taken as a whole and after giving effect to all supplements and updates thereto) contains any material misstatement of fact or omits to state any material fact necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not materially misleading; *provided* that with respect to projected financial information, the Borrower represents only that such information was prepared in good faith based upon assumptions believed by management of the Borrower to be reasonable at the time made, it being understood that projections are not to be viewed as facts and that actual results may differ materially from such projections.

**[Section 4.12 --- Intellectual Property]{.underline}**

The Borrower and each Restricted Subsidiary owns, licenses, or otherwise has the right to use all Intellectual Property reasonably necessary for the conduct of its business as currently conducted, except where the failure to own, license, or have such rights would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect. To the knowledge of the Borrower, the conduct of the business of the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries does not infringe, misappropriate, or otherwise violate the Intellectual Property rights of any other Person in any manner that would reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.

**[Section 4.13 --- Subsidiaries]{.underline}**

Schedule 4.13 sets forth, as of the Closing Date, a true and complete list of each Subsidiary of the Borrower, its jurisdiction of organization, the percentage of issued and outstanding Equity Interests thereof owned by the Borrower and its Subsidiaries, and whether such Subsidiary is a Restricted Subsidiary or an Unrestricted Subsidiary. As of the Closing Date, there are no Unrestricted Subsidiaries.

**[Section 4.14 --- Sanctions; Anti-Corruption]{.underline}**

**(a)** Neither the Borrower nor any Restricted Subsidiary, nor, to the knowledge of the Borrower, any director, officer, employee, agent, or Affiliate of the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary acting or benefiting in any capacity in connection with the Loans or Letters of Credit, is a Sanctioned Person.

**(b)** The Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries are in compliance in all material respects with applicable Sanctions, Anti-Corruption Laws, and all applicable anti-money laundering laws, including the USA PATRIOT Act.

**(c)** No part of the proceeds of the Loans or any Letter of Credit will be used, directly or indirectly, (i) to fund any activities or business of or with any Sanctioned Person or in any Sanctioned Country, except to the extent permitted by Applicable Law, or (ii) in any manner that would result in a violation of Sanctions or Anti-Corruption Laws by any party hereto.

**[Section 4.15 --- Use of Proceeds]{.underline}**

The proceeds of the Loans and the Letters of Credit will be used solely (a) to refinance in full the Existing Credit Facility, (b) to pay fees, costs, and expenses associated with the Transactions, (c) to fund working capital needs of the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries, and (d) for other general corporate purposes of the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries.

**[Section 4.16 --- Security Documents]{.underline}**

The Security Agreement and the other Security Documents, upon execution and delivery thereof by the parties thereto and, to the extent required, the filing or recording thereof or the taking of possession or control as contemplated thereby, create valid and enforceable Liens on the Collateral purported to be covered thereby in favor of the Collateral Agent, for the benefit of the Secured Parties, which Liens are and will be perfected first-priority Liens (subject only to Permitted Liens). All filings and other actions necessary to perfect and protect such Liens have been duly taken, made, or provided for, except for those that may be completed after the Closing Date as permitted by Schedule 5.11.

**[Section 4.17 --- Intercreditor Agreement]{.underline}**

The Borrower has read and understands the terms and provisions of the Intercreditor Agreement, acknowledges that the Obligations and the Liens securing the Obligations are subject thereto, and agrees to be bound by the terms thereof to the extent applicable to it. The execution, delivery, and performance of the Intercreditor Agreement by the Borrower have been duly authorized, and the Intercreditor Agreement constitutes a legal, valid, and binding obligation of the Borrower, enforceable against the Borrower in accordance with its terms, subject to Debtor Relief Laws and general equitable principles.

**[ARTICLE V --- AFFIRMATIVE COVENANTS]{.underline}**

Until the Latest First Lien Maturity Date has occurred and all Obligations (other than contingent indemnification obligations not then due) have been indefeasibly paid in full and the Revolving Commitments have terminated, the Borrower covenants and agrees with the Administrative Agent and the Lenders that:

**[Section 5.01 --- Financial Statements]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall furnish to the Administrative Agent (for prompt distribution to each Lender):

**(a) Annual Financial Statements.** As soon as available, and in any event within 90 days after the end of each fiscal year of the Borrower, commencing with the fiscal year ending December 31, 2025, the audited consolidated balance sheet of the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries as of the end of such fiscal year and the related audited consolidated statements of income, stockholders\' equity, and cash flows for such fiscal year, setting forth in each case in comparative form the figures for the previous fiscal year, all reported on by an independent registered public accounting firm of nationally recognized standing or other independent certified public accountants reasonably acceptable to the Administrative Agent, without any going concern qualification or exception (other than a qualification or exception solely with respect to the maturity of the Loans or any potential inability to satisfy a financial covenant in a future period).

**(b) Quarterly Financial Statements.** As soon as available, and in any event within 45 days after the end of each of the first three fiscal quarters of each fiscal year of the Borrower, commencing with the fiscal quarter ending September 30, 2025, the unaudited consolidated balance sheet of the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries as of the end of such fiscal quarter and the related unaudited consolidated statements of income and cash flows for such fiscal quarter and the portion of the fiscal year then ended, setting forth in each case in comparative form the figures for the corresponding fiscal quarter and corresponding portion of the previous fiscal year, all certified by a Financial Officer of the Borrower as fairly presenting in all material respects the financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows of the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries in accordance with GAAP, subject to normal year-end audit adjustments and the absence of footnotes.

**(c) Delivery Method.** Documents required to be delivered pursuant to this Section 5.01 may be delivered electronically and, if so delivered, shall be deemed to have been delivered on the date (i) on which the Borrower posts such documents, or provides a link thereto, on the Borrower\'s website on the Internet or at any investor relations page on such website to which each Lender and the Administrative Agent has access, or (ii) on which such documents are posted on behalf of the Borrower on an Internet or intranet website, if any, to which each Lender and the Administrative Agent has access.

**[Section 5.02 --- Compliance Certificates; Notices]{.underline}**

**(a) Compliance Certificates.** Together with the delivery of each set of financial statements pursuant to Sections 5.01(a) and 5.01(b), the Borrower shall deliver to the Administrative Agent a Compliance Certificate substantially in the form of Exhibit A, signed by a Financial Officer of the Borrower:

> \(i\) certifying that such Financial Officer has reviewed this Agreement and the other Loan Documents and has made or caused to be made under his or her supervision a review in reasonable detail of the transactions and condition (financial or otherwise) of the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries during the accounting period covered by such financial statements;
>
> \(ii\) certifying as to whether, to the knowledge of such Financial Officer, a Default or Event of Default has occurred and is continuing and, if so, specifying the details thereof and the action that the Borrower has taken or proposes to take with respect thereto;
>
> \(iii\) setting forth calculations in reasonable detail demonstrating compliance with the financial covenants set forth in Section 6.10, including the Total First Lien Net Leverage Ratio and the Interest Coverage Ratio, in each case for the relevant testing period; and
>
> \(iv\) identifying any Prepayment Events during the period covered thereby and the amount of any mandatory prepayments required or anticipated pursuant to Section 2.05.

**(b) Notice of Default.** Promptly, and in any event within five Business Days after any Responsible Officer of the Borrower obtains knowledge thereof, the Borrower shall notify the Administrative Agent in writing of the occurrence of any Default or Event of Default, specifying the nature and extent thereof and the action the Borrower has taken or proposes to take with respect thereto.

**(c) Other Notices.** Promptly, and in any event within five Business Days after any Responsible Officer of the Borrower obtains knowledge thereof, the Borrower shall notify the Administrative Agent in writing of:

> \(i\) any litigation, investigation, or proceeding affecting the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary that would reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect;
>
> \(ii\) any ERISA Event that would reasonably be expected to result in a Material Adverse Effect;
>
> \(iii\) any event, change, or circumstance that has had or would reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect; and
>
> \(iv\) any material change in accounting policies or financial reporting practices.

For the avoidance of doubt, delivery of a Prepayment Event Notice pursuant to Section 5.13 or Section 2.05(b)(v) shall not satisfy the Borrower\'s obligation to deliver a notice of Default or Event of Default under this Section 5.02(b) if the underlying matter also constitutes a Default or Event of Default.

**[Section 5.03 --- Existence; Business]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall, and shall cause each Restricted Subsidiary to, (a) preserve, renew, and maintain in full force and effect its legal existence and good standing under the laws of the jurisdiction of its organization, except in connection with transactions permitted by Section 6.03, and (b) take all reasonable action to maintain all rights, privileges, permits, licenses, and franchises necessary or desirable in the normal conduct of its business, except, in the case of clause (b), where the failure to do so would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.

**[Section 5.04 --- Payment of Obligations]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall, and shall cause each Restricted Subsidiary to, pay, discharge, or otherwise satisfy as the same shall become due and payable, all of its material obligations and liabilities, including Tax liabilities, before the same shall become delinquent or in default, except (a) where the validity or amount thereof is being contested in good faith by appropriate proceedings and adequate reserves are maintained in accordance with GAAP or (b) where the failure to do so would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.

**[Section 5.05 --- Insurance]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall, and shall cause each Restricted Subsidiary to, maintain, with financially sound and reputable insurance companies, insurance in such amounts, with such deductibles, and covering such risks as is customarily maintained by companies of similar size engaged in similar businesses and as may be required by any Governmental Authority or by any material contractual obligation. Without limiting the foregoing:

**(a)** the Borrower shall cause the Administrative Agent, for the benefit of the Secured Parties, to be named as lender loss payee on all property insurance policies covering Collateral and as additional insured on all liability insurance policies of the Loan Parties, in each case to the extent customary and available on commercially reasonable terms;

**(b)** the Borrower shall furnish to the Administrative Agent from time to time upon reasonable request information in reasonable detail as to the insurance so maintained; and

**(c)** all proceeds of insurance and condemnation awards constituting Net Cash Proceeds shall be applied in accordance with Section 2.05(b) and the Intercreditor Agreement.

**[Section 5.06 --- Books and Records; Inspection]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall, and shall cause each Restricted Subsidiary to, maintain proper books of record and account, in which entries are made that are full, true, and correct in all material respects and that permit the preparation of financial statements in accordance with GAAP. The Borrower shall, and shall cause each Restricted Subsidiary to, permit any representatives designated by the Administrative Agent or, during the continuance of an Event of Default, any Lender, upon reasonable prior notice and during normal business hours, to visit and inspect its properties, to examine and make extracts from its books and records, and to discuss its affairs, finances, and condition with its officers and, if an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing, its independent accountants, all at such reasonable times and as often as reasonably requested; *provided* that, absent an Event of Default, such inspections shall occur not more than once during any fiscal year and the Borrower shall only be obligated to reimburse the reasonable and documented out-of-pocket costs and expenses of the Administrative Agent for one such inspection in any fiscal year.

**[Section 5.07 --- Compliance with Laws]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall, and shall cause each Restricted Subsidiary to, comply in all material respects with all Applicable Laws, including Environmental Laws, ERISA, Anti-Corruption Laws, Sanctions, and all laws relating to labor, health and safety, and zoning, except where the failure to do so would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.

**[Section 5.08 --- Environmental]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall, and shall cause each Restricted Subsidiary to:

**(a)** comply in all material respects with all Environmental Laws and obtain and maintain all material permits, licenses, and approvals required thereunder for their operations and properties;

**(b)** promptly notify the Administrative Agent of any pending or threatened Environmental Claim, any release of Hazardous Materials, or any violation of Environmental Laws, in each case that would reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect; and

**(c)** conduct and complete all investigations, studies, sampling, and testing, and all remedial, removal, and other actions required under Environmental Laws and promptly comply in all material respects with all lawful orders and directives of all Governmental Authorities regarding Environmental Laws, except where the same are being contested in good faith by appropriate proceedings and adequate reserves are maintained in accordance with GAAP.

For purposes of this Section 5.08, \"**Environmental Claim**\" means any written claim, action, cause of action, investigation, notice of violation, or proceeding by any Person alleging potential liability under or violation of any Environmental Law.

**[Section 5.09 --- Additional Guarantors and Collateral]{.underline}**

If, after the Closing Date, the Borrower forms or acquires any new Domestic Subsidiary that is a Restricted Subsidiary and is not an Excluded Subsidiary, then the Borrower shall, within 60 days after such formation or acquisition (or such longer period as the Administrative Agent may agree in its reasonable discretion), cause such Domestic Subsidiary to:

**(a)** execute and deliver to the Administrative Agent a Joinder Agreement pursuant to which such Domestic Subsidiary becomes a Guarantor under Article IX and a grantor under the Security Agreement;

**(b)** deliver to the Administrative Agent customary organizational documents, resolutions, good standing certificates, incumbency certificates, and opinions of counsel (if reasonably requested by the Administrative Agent);

**(c)** grant Liens to the Collateral Agent on substantially all of its personal property assets constituting Collateral, subject to Excluded Assets and Permitted Liens;

**(d)** if such Domestic Subsidiary owns any Material Real Property, execute and deliver a Mortgage and such surveys, title insurance policies, appraisals, and environmental reports as the Administrative Agent may reasonably request, subject to customary thresholds and timing; and

**(e)** take all actions reasonably requested by the Administrative Agent to perfect the Liens granted pursuant to the Security Documents.

**[Section 5.10 --- Use of Proceeds]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall use the proceeds of the Loans and the Letters of Credit solely for the purposes specified in Section 4.15 and not for any purpose prohibited by Applicable Law or by any Loan Document.

**[Section 5.11 --- Post-Closing Matters]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall, and shall cause the other Loan Parties to, deliver, execute, file, or record, as applicable, each of the items and documents set forth on Schedule 5.11 within the time periods specified therein (or such later date as the Administrative Agent may agree in writing in its reasonable discretion), including mortgage recordings, insurance endorsements, local counsel opinions, control agreements, and other perfection or collateral deliverables. Failure to timely satisfy any material post-closing obligation set forth on Schedule 5.11 shall constitute a Default, subject to any express grace periods set forth therein.

**[Section 5.12 --- ERISA Matters]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall, and shall cause each ERISA Affiliate to, maintain compliance in all material respects with ERISA and the Code with respect to each Plan and each Multiemployer Plan. The Borrower shall promptly, and in any event within 10 Business Days after any Responsible Officer of the Borrower obtains knowledge thereof, notify the Administrative Agent in writing of the occurrence of any ERISA Event that would reasonably be expected to result in liability to the Borrower or any ERISA Affiliate in excess of \$10,000,000.

**[Section 5.13 --- Prepayment Event Notices; Reporting]{.underline}**

Without limiting the requirements of Section 2.05(b)(v), the Borrower shall deliver to the Administrative Agent a Prepayment Event Notice in accordance with Section 2.05(b)(v) upon the occurrence of any Asset Disposition or Casualty Event yielding Net Cash Proceeds in excess of the Reporting Threshold of \$3,000,000. Such notice shall be delivered whether or not such event independently triggers an immediate mandatory prepayment under Section 2.05(b)(i), and shall include updates if the estimated Net Cash Proceeds or expected timing of receipt materially changes.

**[ARTICLE VI --- NEGATIVE COVENANTS]{.underline}**

Until the Latest First Lien Maturity Date has occurred and all Obligations (other than contingent indemnification obligations not then due) have been indefeasibly paid in full and the Revolving Commitments have terminated, the Borrower covenants and agrees with the Administrative Agent and the Lenders that:

**[Section 6.01 --- Indebtedness]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall not, and shall not permit any Restricted Subsidiary to, create, incur, assume, or permit to exist any Indebtedness, except:

**(a)** the Obligations;

**(b)** the Second Lien Obligations under the Second Lien Credit Agreement and the other Second Lien Loan Documents in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed \$150,000,000 at any time outstanding, so long as such Indebtedness is subject in all respects to the Intercreditor Agreement;

**(c)** Indebtedness set forth on Schedule 6.01 and any Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness in respect thereof;

**(d)** intercompany Indebtedness among the Borrower and the Restricted Subsidiaries to the extent not prohibited by the other Loan Documents; *provided* that any such Indebtedness owed by a Loan Party to a non-Loan Party shall be subordinated to the Obligations on terms reasonably satisfactory to the Administrative Agent if requested by the Administrative Agent after the occurrence and during the continuance of an Event of Default;

**(e)** purchase money Indebtedness and Capital Lease Obligations incurred to finance the acquisition, lease, construction, repair, replacement, or improvement of fixed or capital assets in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed \$20,000,000 at any time outstanding;

**(f)** obligations under Hedging Agreements entered into in the ordinary course of business and not for speculative purposes;

**(g)** reimbursement obligations in respect of letters of credit, bank guaranties, surety bonds, performance bonds, and similar obligations issued in the ordinary course of business, in each case not constituting Indebtedness for borrowed money;

**(h)** earn-outs, purchase price adjustments, and similar contingent obligations incurred in connection with Permitted Acquisitions and other Investments permitted hereunder;

**(i)** Guarantees by the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary of Indebtedness otherwise permitted under this Section 6.01; *provided* that if the Indebtedness being Guaranteed is subordinated or junior in right of payment to the Obligations, then such Guarantee shall be subordinated or junior in right of payment to the Obligations to at least the same extent; and

**(j)** additional unsecured Indebtedness or subordinated Indebtedness of the Borrower and the Guarantors in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed \$25,000,000 at any time outstanding; *provided* that (i) no Default or Event of Default shall have occurred and be continuing or would result therefrom, (ii) such Indebtedness shall have no scheduled maturity, mandatory amortization, or sinking fund payments prior to the date that is 91 days after the Latest First Lien Maturity Date, and (iii) in the case of subordinated Indebtedness, the terms thereof shall be reasonably satisfactory to the Administrative Agent.

All Indebtedness permitted by this Section 6.01 is referred to herein as \"**Permitted Indebtedness**.\"

**[Section 6.02 --- Liens]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall not, and shall not permit any Restricted Subsidiary to, create, incur, assume, or permit to exist any Lien on any property or asset now owned or hereafter acquired by it, except:

**(a)** Liens created under the Loan Documents securing the First Lien Obligations;

**(b)** Liens securing the Second Lien Obligations, so long as such Liens are junior to the Liens securing the Obligations and subject in all respects to the Intercreditor Agreement;

**(c)** Liens listed on Schedule 6.02 and any renewals, extensions, or replacements thereof that do not extend to additional property and do not secure a greater principal amount than the Indebtedness so secured as of the Closing Date (except by an amount equal to accrued interest, fees, premiums, and expenses);

**(d)** Permitted Liens arising by operation of law, including carriers\', warehousemen\'s, landlords\', mechanics\', repairmen\'s, materialmen\'s, and other like Liens, in each case incurred in the ordinary course of business and securing obligations that are not overdue by more than 30 days or that are being contested in good faith by appropriate proceedings and for which adequate reserves have been established in accordance with GAAP;

**(e)** pledges and deposits made in the ordinary course of business in compliance with workers\' compensation, unemployment insurance, old-age pensions, and other social security laws or regulations or to secure bids, trade contracts, leases, statutory obligations, surety and appeal bonds, performance bonds, and other obligations of a like nature;

**(f)** easements, rights-of-way, restrictions, encroachments, covenants, reservations, zoning restrictions, and other similar encumbrances on Real Property that do not, individually or in the aggregate, materially interfere with the ordinary conduct of the business of the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries;

**(g)** Liens securing purchase money Indebtedness and Capital Lease Obligations permitted by Section 6.01(e); *provided* that such Liens attach only to the property acquired, leased, or improved with the proceeds of such Indebtedness and the proceeds and products thereof;

**(h)** banker's Liens, rights of setoff, and similar Liens existing solely with respect to cash and Cash Equivalents on deposit in one or more accounts, in each case granted in the ordinary course of business in favor of the bank or banks with which such accounts are maintained;

**(i)** non-exclusive licenses of Intellectual Property granted in the ordinary course of business; and

**(j)** customary precautionary UCC financing statements filed with respect to operating leases, consignments, or sale and leaseback transactions permitted hereunder.

All Liens permitted by this Section 6.02 are referred to herein as \"**Permitted Liens**.\"

**[Section 6.03 --- Fundamental Changes]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall not, and shall not permit any Restricted Subsidiary to, merge into or consolidate with any other Person, or liquidate, wind up, or dissolve itself (or suffer any liquidation or dissolution), or dispose of all or substantially all of its business or assets, except that:

**(a)** any Restricted Subsidiary may merge into the Borrower; *provided* that the Borrower shall be the surviving entity;

**(b)** any Restricted Subsidiary may merge into or consolidate with any other Restricted Subsidiary; *provided* that if any party to such merger or consolidation is a Guarantor, the surviving entity shall be a Guarantor;

**(c)** any Restricted Subsidiary may liquidate or dissolve if the Borrower determines in good faith that such liquidation or dissolution is in the best interests of the Borrower and is not materially disadvantageous to the Lenders; *provided* that any assets of such Restricted Subsidiary are transferred to the Borrower or another Restricted Subsidiary in compliance with this Agreement;

**(d)** the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary may consummate any Permitted Acquisition; and

**(e)** the Borrower may merge with another Person in connection with a transaction otherwise permitted hereunder; *provided* that (i) the Borrower shall be the surviving entity or, if another Person is the surviving entity, such surviving entity shall expressly assume the Obligations and shall be organized under the laws of the United States, any state thereof, or the District of Columbia, (ii) no Default or Event of Default shall have occurred and be continuing or would result therefrom, and (iii) the Borrower shall have delivered to the Administrative Agent such documents as the Administrative Agent may reasonably request to evidence compliance with this Section 6.03.

**[Section 6.04 --- Asset Dispositions]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall not, and shall not permit any Restricted Subsidiary to, consummate any Asset Disposition, unless:

**(a)** the consideration received for such Asset Disposition is at least equal to the fair market value of the assets so disposed of, as determined in good faith by the Borrower;

**(b)** at least 75% of the consideration received by the Borrower or such Restricted Subsidiary in respect of such Asset Disposition shall be in the form of cash or Cash Equivalents; *provided* that the amount of any liabilities of the Borrower or such Restricted Subsidiary (as shown on the Borrower\'s or such Restricted Subsidiary\'s most recent balance sheet or in the notes thereto) that are assumed by the transferee of any such assets and from which the Borrower and all Restricted Subsidiaries have been validly released shall be deemed to be cash for purposes of this clause;

**(c)** the Net Cash Proceeds thereof are applied in accordance with Section 2.05(b) and the Intercreditor Agreement;

**(d)** the Borrower complies with the anti-evasion provisions set forth in Section 2.05(b)(iii), which are hereby incorporated by reference into this Section 6.04 as if set forth in full herein; and

**(e)** with respect to any Asset Disposition yielding Net Cash Proceeds in excess of the Reporting Threshold of \$3,000,000, the Borrower delivers a Prepayment Event Notice in accordance with Section 2.05(b)(v) and Section 5.13.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, the restrictions in this Section 6.04 shall not apply to Asset Dispositions otherwise excluded from the definition of Asset Disposition.

**[Section 6.05 --- Restricted Payments]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall not, and shall not permit any Restricted Subsidiary to, declare or make, directly or indirectly, any Restricted Payment, except:

**(a)** Restricted Subsidiaries may make dividends and distributions to the Borrower or any Guarantor and, if a Restricted Subsidiary is not a wholly-owned Subsidiary, to minority equity holders thereof ratably based on their ownership interests;

**(b)** the Borrower and the Restricted Subsidiaries may make Restricted Payments to purchase, redeem, retire, or otherwise acquire Equity Interests of the Borrower held by current or former employees, officers, directors, managers, or consultants (or their estates, spouses, former spouses, or other permitted transferees) pursuant to employee equity plans, management equity arrangements, stock option plans, or other agreements approved by the Board, in an aggregate amount not to exceed \$7,500,000 in any fiscal year;

**(c)** the Borrower may make Restricted Payments in an amount not to exceed the aggregate amount of cash proceeds received from contributions to its common equity or from the issuance of Qualified Equity Interests after the Closing Date;

**(d)** the Borrower may make other Restricted Payments so long as the Payment Conditions are satisfied; and

**(e)** the Borrower may pay dividends solely in Qualified Equity Interests.

No Restricted Payment shall be made under clause (d) above if a Default or Event of Default has occurred and is continuing or would result therefrom.

**[Section 6.06 --- Investments]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall not, and shall not permit any Restricted Subsidiary to, make or hold any Investment, except:

**(a)** Investments existing on the Closing Date and set forth on Schedule 4.13;

**(b)** Cash Equivalents;

**(c)** intercompany loans, advances, and Investments among the Borrower and the Restricted Subsidiaries to the extent not prohibited by this Agreement;

**(d)** Permitted Acquisitions;

**(e)** accounts receivable, trade credit, advances to customers, and loans or advances to employees, officers, directors, managers, consultants, and suppliers, in each case made in the ordinary course of business;

**(f)** Investments constituting prepaid expenses, negotiable instruments held for collection, security deposits, lease deposits, utility deposits, and similar deposits entered into in the ordinary course of business;

**(g)** Hedging Agreements permitted by Section 6.01(f);

**(h)** Investments received in connection with the bankruptcy or reorganization of suppliers and customers or in settlement of delinquent obligations of, and other disputes with, customers and suppliers arising in the ordinary course of business; and

**(i)** other Investments in an aggregate amount not to exceed \$20,000,000 at any time outstanding; *provided* that with respect to any such Investment in excess of \$10,000,000, the Payment Conditions shall be satisfied on a Pro Forma Basis.

All Investments permitted by this Section 6.06 are referred to herein as \"**Permitted Investments**.\"

The Borrower may designate any Subsidiary as an Unrestricted Subsidiary only if (i) no Default or Event of Default has occurred and is continuing or would result therefrom, (ii) such Subsidiary has no Indebtedness other than non-recourse Indebtedness, (iii) such designation would be permitted as an Investment under this Section 6.06, and (iv) the Borrower delivers to the Administrative Agent a certificate of a Financial Officer certifying compliance with the foregoing.

**[Section 6.07 --- Transactions with Affiliates]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall not, and shall not permit any Restricted Subsidiary to, enter into any transaction, arrangement, or series of related transactions with any Affiliate (other than the Borrower and wholly-owned Restricted Subsidiaries) involving aggregate consideration in excess of \$2,500,000 unless such transaction, arrangement, or series of related transactions is on terms no less favorable to the Borrower or such Restricted Subsidiary than would be obtainable in a comparable arm\'s-length transaction with a Person that is not an Affiliate. Any such transaction or arrangement involving aggregate consideration in excess of \$10,000,000 shall require approval by a majority of the disinterested members of the Board (or other governing body) of the Borrower.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, the following shall be permitted: (a) transactions among the Borrower and its wholly-owned Restricted Subsidiaries, (b) reasonable and customary compensation and reimbursement arrangements for directors, officers, employees, consultants, and managers, (c) transactions pursuant to employee benefit and incentive compensation plans approved by the Board, and (d) Restricted Payments permitted by Section 6.05.

**[Section 6.08 --- Restrictive Agreements]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall not, and shall not permit any Restricted Subsidiary to, enter into or permit to exist any agreement, instrument, deed, lease, or other arrangement that prohibits, restricts, or imposes any condition upon:

**(a)** the ability of any Restricted Subsidiary to pay dividends or other distributions to the Borrower or any Guarantor, or to make or repay loans or advances to the Borrower or any Guarantor;

**(b)** the ability of any Restricted Subsidiary to create, incur, assume, or permit to exist Liens on property of such Restricted Subsidiary in favor of the Collateral Agent for the benefit of the Secured Parties; or

**(c)** the ability of any Restricted Subsidiary to Guarantee the Obligations;

except for:

> \(i\) restrictions and conditions imposed by Applicable Law;
>
> \(ii\) restrictions and conditions existing on the Closing Date and identified on Schedule 6.01 or Schedule 6.02 (but not any extension or renewal thereof that expands the scope of such restriction or condition);
>
> \(iii\) restrictions and conditions contained in this Agreement, the other Loan Documents, the Second Lien Loan Documents (to the extent permitted by Section 6.09 and the Intercreditor Agreement), and any documents governing Indebtedness permitted under Section 6.01(e) that apply only to the assets financed thereby;
>
> \(iv\) customary restrictions and conditions contained in agreements relating to the sale of a Restricted Subsidiary or assets pending such sale, provided such restrictions and conditions apply only to the Restricted Subsidiary or assets to be sold and such sale is permitted hereunder;
>
> \(v\) customary restrictions and conditions in leases, licenses, or similar contracts entered into in the ordinary course of business; and
>
> \(vi\) restrictions in Organizational Documents of joint ventures and non-wholly-owned Subsidiaries that apply solely to such Persons.

**[Section 6.09 --- Amendments to Second Lien Documents]{.underline}**

The Borrower shall not, and shall not permit any Restricted Subsidiary to, amend, modify, supplement, waive, or otherwise change, or consent to any amendment, modification, supplement, waiver, or other change to, any provision of the Second Lien Credit Agreement or any other Second Lien Loan Document if the effect of such amendment, modification, supplement, waiver, or change would be materially adverse to the interests of the Administrative Agent or the First Lien Lenders, unless such amendment, modification, supplement, waiver, or change is consented to in writing by the Administrative Agent (acting at the direction of the Required Lenders, to the extent such direction is required under this Agreement) or is expressly permitted under the Intercreditor Agreement, including the provisions of Section 2.03(c) thereof regarding Permitted Second Lien Refinancing Indebtedness.

Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Borrower shall not permit any amendment to the Second Lien Credit Agreement or any other Second Lien Loan Document that would:

**(a)** shorten the final maturity of the Second Lien Obligations to a date earlier than 91 days after the Latest First Lien Maturity Date then in effect;

**(b)** increase the principal amount of the Second Lien Obligations above the amount permitted under Section 6.01, except for capitalized interest, premiums, fees, and customary refinancing costs permitted under the Intercreditor Agreement;

**(c)** grant any Lien securing the Second Lien Obligations on any assets other than assets constituting Collateral securing the First Lien Obligations, except as otherwise permitted by the Intercreditor Agreement; or

**(d)** alter the subordination, turnover, standstill, or lien priority provisions of the Intercreditor Agreement in a manner adverse to the First Lien Lenders.

**[Section 6.10 --- Financial Covenants]{.underline}**

**(a) Total First Lien Net Leverage Ratio.** The Borrower shall not permit the Total First Lien Net Leverage Ratio, determined as of the last day of each fiscal quarter of the Borrower commencing with the fiscal quarter ending September 30, 2025, for the period of four consecutive fiscal quarters then ended, to exceed 5.50:1.00.

For illustrative purposes only, the Total First Lien Net Leverage Ratio as of the Closing Date, based on \$475,000,000 of Total First Lien Facility commitments and LTM EBITDA of \$89,400,000, is approximately 5.31:1.00 before giving effect to any unrestricted cash netting.

**(b) Interest Coverage Ratio.** The Borrower shall not permit the ratio of Consolidated EBITDA to consolidated cash interest expense, in each case for the period of four consecutive fiscal quarters then ended, determined as of the last day of each fiscal quarter of the Borrower commencing with the fiscal quarter ending September 30, 2025, to be less than 2.00:1.00.

**(c) Definitions.** All capitalized terms used in this Section 6.10 and not otherwise defined herein shall have the meanings assigned thereto in Section 1.01, and all financial covenant calculations shall be made in accordance with Article I.

**[ARTICLE VII --- EVENTS OF DEFAULT]{.underline}**

**[Section 7.01 --- Events of Default]{.underline}**

Each of the following shall constitute an \"**Event of Default**\":

**(a) Non-Payment of Principal.** The Borrower shall fail to pay any principal of any Loan or any reimbursement obligation in respect of any LC Disbursement when and as the same shall become due and payable, whether at the due date thereof, at a date fixed for prepayment thereof, at maturity, by acceleration, or otherwise.

**(b) Non-Payment of Interest and Other Amounts.** The Borrower shall fail to pay any interest on any Loan, any fee, or any other amount (other than principal) payable under this Agreement or any other Loan Document, within five Business Days after the same becomes due and payable.

**(c) Failure to Deliver Required Notices or Certificates.** The Borrower shall fail to deliver any Prepayment Event Notice required under Section 2.05(b)(v) or Section 5.13, or any Compliance Certificate required under Section 5.02, within 30 days after the Borrower becomes aware of such failure.

**(d) Misrepresentations.** Any representation, warranty, certification, or statement made or deemed made by or on behalf of the Borrower or any other Loan Party in this Agreement, any other Loan Document, or any report, certificate, financial statement, or other document furnished in connection herewith or therewith shall prove to have been materially untrue or misleading when made or deemed made.

**(e) Covenant Defaults.** The Borrower shall fail to observe or perform:

> \(i\) any covenant, condition, or agreement contained in Sections 5.01, 5.02, or 5.03 or in Article VI, and such failure shall continue for 30 days after written notice thereof from the Administrative Agent to the Borrower or from the Required Lenders to the Borrower and the Administrative Agent; *provided* that (A) any failure to comply with the financial covenants set forth in Section 6.10 shall constitute an immediate Event of Default without grace or notice, and (B) any failure to comply with Sections 6.01 through 6.05 shall constitute an immediate Event of Default without grace or notice; or
>
> \(ii\) any other covenant, condition, or agreement contained in this Agreement or any other Loan Document, and such failure shall continue unremedied for 30 days after written notice thereof from the Administrative Agent to the Borrower or from the Required Lenders to the Borrower and the Administrative Agent.

**(f) Cross-Default.** The Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary shall fail to make any payment when due (after giving effect to any applicable grace period) in respect of any Material Indebtedness (other than the Obligations) in an aggregate principal amount exceeding \$10,000,000, or any event or condition shall occur under any such Material Indebtedness if the effect of such event or condition is to cause, or permit the holder or holders thereof (or a trustee or agent on behalf of such holder or holders) to cause, such Material Indebtedness to become due prior to its stated maturity or to require the purchase, redemption, defeasance, or repurchase thereof prior to its stated maturity.

For purposes of this Section 7.01(f), \"**Material Indebtedness**\" means Indebtedness (other than the Obligations) of the Borrower and/or one or more Restricted Subsidiaries in an aggregate principal amount exceeding \$10,000,000.

**(g) Judgments.** One or more judgments, orders, or decrees for the payment of money in an aggregate amount exceeding \$10,000,000 (to the extent not covered by independent third-party insurance as to which the insurer has not denied coverage) shall be rendered against the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary and the same shall remain undischarged, unvacated, unbonded, or unstayed for a period of 60 consecutive days.

**(h) Bankruptcy or Insolvency.** Any of the following shall occur: the Borrower or any Material Restricted Subsidiary shall (i) commence any voluntary case or proceeding under any Debtor Relief Law, (ii) consent to the entry of an order for relief against it in an involuntary case or proceeding under any Debtor Relief Law, (iii) consent to the appointment of a receiver, trustee, custodian, sequestrator, conservator, or similar official for it or for all or substantially all of its assets, (iv) make a general assignment for the benefit of creditors, or (v) become unable or admit in writing its inability generally to pay its debts as they become due; or an involuntary case or proceeding shall be commenced against the Borrower or any Material Restricted Subsidiary under any Debtor Relief Law seeking liquidation, reorganization, or other relief, and such case or proceeding shall remain undismissed or unstayed for a period of 60 consecutive days, or an order for relief shall be entered therein.

**(i) ERISA Events.** An ERISA Event shall have occurred that, together with all other ERISA Events that have occurred, would reasonably be expected to result in liability of the Borrower and its ERISA Affiliates in an aggregate amount exceeding \$10,000,000.

**(j) Change of Control.** A Change of Control shall occur.

**(k) Invalidity of Loan Documents or Liens.** Any Loan Document at any time after its execution and delivery and for any reason other than as expressly permitted hereunder or thereunder, or as a result of payment in full of all Obligations and termination of the Commitments, shall cease to be in full force and effect, or shall be declared null and void, or the Borrower or any other Loan Party shall contest in writing the validity or enforceability of any Loan Document, or deny in writing that it has any further liability or obligation thereunder, or any Lien created by the Security Documents on any material portion of the Collateral shall cease to be a valid and perfected first-priority Lien (subject to Permitted Liens), except to the extent that any such loss of perfection or priority results from the failure of the Administrative Agent to maintain possession of certificates actually delivered to it representing securities pledged under the Security Documents or to file UCC continuation statements.

**(l) Intercreditor Agreement Breach.** The Borrower shall breach or fail to perform any material provision of the Intercreditor Agreement applicable to it, and such breach or failure shall be materially adverse to the interests of the First Lien Lenders.

**[Section 7.02 --- Remedies upon Event of Default]{.underline}**

**(a) General Remedies.** If any Event of Default shall occur and be continuing, then and in every such event, the Administrative Agent may, and at the request of the Required Lenders shall, by notice to the Borrower, take any or all of the following actions, at the same or different times:

> \(i\) terminate the Revolving Commitments, whereupon the Revolving Commitments shall terminate immediately;
>
> \(ii\) declare the principal of and any accrued interest on the Loans, and all other Obligations owing hereunder and under the other Loan Documents, to be, whereupon the same shall become, immediately due and payable without presentment, demand, protest, or other notice of any kind, all of which are hereby waived by the Borrower;
>
> \(iii\) require the Borrower to cash collateralize the LC Exposure in an amount equal to 103% of the aggregate LC Exposure, and if the Borrower fails forthwith to deposit such amount in cash, the Administrative Agent may proceed to enforce any or all Liens and other rights under the Security Documents; and
>
> \(iv\) exercise all rights and remedies available to the Administrative Agent, the Issuing Bank, and the Lenders under the Loan Documents, Applicable Law, or otherwise.

**(b) Automatic Remedies.** In the case of an Event of Default specified in Section 7.01(h) with respect to the Borrower, automatically and without any action by the Administrative Agent, the Issuing Bank, or any Lender, the Revolving Commitments shall immediately terminate and the principal of the Loans then outstanding, together with accrued interest thereon and all fees and other Obligations, shall immediately become due and payable, and the Borrower shall immediately be required to cash collateralize the LC Exposure in an amount equal to 103% thereof.

**(c) Intercreditor Limitation.** All rights and remedies of the Administrative Agent, the Issuing Bank, and the Lenders under this Agreement and the other Loan Documents shall be exercised subject to the terms of the Intercreditor Agreement, including any applicable standstill, turnover, or other limitation on enforcement or exercise of remedies set forth therein.

**(d) Default Rate.** Upon the occurrence and during the continuance of any Event of Default, the Default Rate shall automatically apply to all outstanding Obligations in accordance with Section 2.08(b), without the necessity of further notice or demand.

**[Section 7.03 --- Application of Proceeds after Acceleration]{.underline}**

After the occurrence and during the continuance of an Event of Default and following acceleration of the Loans, all amounts collected or received by the Administrative Agent from the exercise of remedies under the Loan Documents, from the sale or other disposition of Collateral, or otherwise in respect of the Obligations shall be applied, subject in all respects to the Intercreditor Agreement, in the following order of priority:

**(a)** *first*, to the payment of all reasonable and documented costs and expenses incurred by the Administrative Agent in connection with such collection, sale, or other realization, including reasonable and documented fees, charges, and disbursements of counsel and any other agents or advisors engaged by the Administrative Agent;

**(b)** *second*, to the payment of accrued and unpaid interest on the Obligations, including Default Rate interest;

**(c)** *third*, to the payment of the outstanding principal amount of the Term Loans and Revolving Loans;

**(d)** *fourth*, to cash collateralize the LC Exposure at 103% of the aggregate amount thereof;

**(e)** *fifth*, to the payment of all other First Lien Obligations then due and payable; and

**(f)** *sixth*, the balance, if any, to the Borrower or such other Persons as may be lawfully entitled thereto or as a court of competent jurisdiction may direct.

For the avoidance of doubt, the foregoing waterfall is subject to, and shall be interpreted consistently with, the payment waterfall, turnover, and lien priority provisions set forth in the Intercreditor Agreement, and all First Lien Obligations must be indefeasibly paid in full and the Revolving Commitments terminated before any payment may be made on account of any Second Lien Obligations except as expressly permitted by the Intercreditor Agreement.

**[ARTICLE VIII --- THE ADMINISTRATIVE AGENT]{.underline}**

**[Section 8.01 --- Appointment and Authority]{.underline}**

Each of the Lenders and the Issuing Bank hereby irrevocably appoints Cartwright National Bank, N.A. to act on its behalf as the Administrative Agent and the Collateral Agent hereunder and under the other Loan Documents and authorizes the Administrative Agent to take such actions on its behalf and to exercise such powers as are delegated to the Administrative Agent by the terms hereof or thereof, together with such actions and powers as are reasonably incidental thereto. The provisions of this Article VIII are solely for the benefit of the Administrative Agent, the Issuing Bank, and the Lenders, and neither the Borrower nor any other Loan Party shall have rights as a third-party beneficiary of any of such provisions, except as expressly set forth herein.

The Administrative Agent shall hold any security interest created under the Security Documents and any rights granted under the Guaranty for the ratable benefit of the persons entitled thereto (collectively, the \"**Secured Parties**\").

**[Section 8.02 --- Rights as a Lender]{.underline}**

The Person serving as the Administrative Agent shall have the same rights and powers in its capacity as a Lender as any other Lender and may exercise the same as though it were not the Administrative Agent, and the term \"Lender\" or \"Lenders\" shall, unless otherwise expressly indicated or unless the context otherwise requires, include the Person serving as the Administrative Agent in its individual capacity. Such Person and its Affiliates may accept deposits from, lend money to, own securities of, act as the financial advisor or in any other advisory capacity for, and generally engage in any kind of business with, the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any Affiliate thereof as if such Person were not the Administrative Agent hereunder and without any duty to account therefor to the Lenders.

**[Section 8.03 --- Exculpatory Provisions]{.underline}**

The Administrative Agent shall not have any duties or obligations except those expressly set forth herein and in the other Loan Documents. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing:

**(a)** the Administrative Agent shall not be subject to any fiduciary or other implied duties, regardless of whether a Default or Event of Default has occurred and is continuing;

**(b)** the Administrative Agent shall not have any duty to take any discretionary action or exercise any discretionary powers, except discretionary rights and powers expressly contemplated hereby or by the other Loan Documents that the Administrative Agent is required to exercise as directed in writing by the Required Lenders or such other number or percentage of Lenders as shall be expressly provided for herein; *provided* that the Administrative Agent shall not be required to take any action that, in its opinion or the opinion of its counsel, may expose the Administrative Agent to liability or that is contrary to any Loan Document or Applicable Law; and

**(c)** except as expressly set forth herein and in the other Loan Documents, the Administrative Agent shall not have any duty to disclose, and shall not be liable for the failure to disclose, any information relating to the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any Affiliate thereof that is communicated to or obtained by the Person serving as the Administrative Agent or any of its Affiliates in any capacity.

The Administrative Agent shall not be liable for any action taken or not taken by it under or in connection with this Agreement or any other Loan Document or the Transactions, except to the extent that such action or inaction is finally determined by a court of competent jurisdiction in a final and non-appealable judgment to have resulted from the Administrative Agent\'s gross negligence or willful misconduct. The Administrative Agent shall not be responsible for or have any duty to ascertain, inquire into, or verify (i) any statement, warranty, or representation made in or in connection with this Agreement or any other Loan Document, (ii) the contents of any certificate, report, or other document delivered hereunder or thereunder or in connection herewith or therewith, (iii) the performance or observance of any covenant, agreement, or other term or condition set forth herein or therein or the occurrence of any Default, (iv) the validity, enforceability, effectiveness, or genuineness of this Agreement, any other Loan Document, or any other agreement, instrument, or document, or (v) the satisfaction of any condition precedent set forth in Article III or elsewhere herein, other than to confirm receipt of items expressly required to be delivered to the Administrative Agent.

**[Section 8.04 --- Reliance by Administrative Agent]{.underline}**

The Administrative Agent shall be entitled to rely, and shall be fully protected in relying, upon any writing, communication, signature, resolution, representation, notice, consent, certificate, affidavit, letter, telecopy, electronic mail, or other document or conversation believed by it to be genuine and correct and to have been signed, sent, or made by the proper Person, and upon advice and statements of legal counsel (including counsel to the Borrower), independent accountants, and other experts selected by the Administrative Agent. The Administrative Agent may deem and treat the payee of any Note or the person listed as the owner of a Loan or Commitment in the Register as the owner thereof for all purposes unless an Assignment and Assumption shall have become effective in accordance with Section 10.05.

**[Section 8.05 --- Delegation of Duties]{.underline}**

The Administrative Agent may perform any and all of its duties and exercise its rights and powers hereunder or under any other Loan Document by or through any one or more sub-agents appointed by the Administrative Agent. The Administrative Agent and any such sub-agent may perform any and all of its duties and exercise its rights and powers through their respective Related Parties. The exculpatory provisions of this Article VIII shall apply to any such sub-agent and to the Related Parties of the Administrative Agent and any such sub-agent.

**[Section 8.06 --- Resignation or Removal]{.underline}**

**(a) Resignation.** The Administrative Agent may at any time give notice of its resignation to the Lenders, the Issuing Bank, and the Borrower. Upon receipt of any such notice of resignation, the Required Lenders shall have the right, with the consent of the Borrower (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed unless an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing), to appoint a successor, which shall be a bank or trust company with an office in the United States, or an Affiliate of any such bank or trust company, reasonably experienced in serving as administrative agent in syndicated credit facilities of the type contemplated hereby. If no such successor shall have been so appointed by the Required Lenders and shall have accepted such appointment within 30 days after the retiring Administrative Agent gives notice of its resignation, then the retiring Administrative Agent may, on behalf of the Lenders and the Issuing Bank, appoint a successor Administrative Agent meeting the qualifications set forth above; *provided* that if the Administrative Agent shall notify the Borrower and the Lenders that no qualifying Person has accepted such appointment, then such resignation shall nonetheless become effective in accordance with this Section 8.06.

**(b) Removal for Cause.** The Required Lenders may remove the Administrative Agent for cause by written notice to the Administrative Agent, the Borrower, and the Issuing Bank. For purposes hereof, \"cause\" shall mean the Administrative Agent\'s gross negligence, willful misconduct, material breach of its express obligations under this Agreement, or a Bankruptcy Event affecting the Administrative Agent.

**(c) Effectiveness.** With effect from the effective date of the resignation or removal of the Administrative Agent and the appointment of a successor Administrative Agent, (i) such successor shall succeed to and become vested with all of the rights, powers, privileges, and duties of the retiring or removed Administrative Agent, and (ii) the retiring or removed Administrative Agent shall be discharged from its duties and obligations under the Loan Documents. The fees payable by the Borrower to a successor Administrative Agent shall be those agreed between the Borrower and such successor. Upon the acceptance of its appointment as Administrative Agent hereunder by a successor, such successor shall deliver notice thereof to the Lenders, the Issuing Bank, and the Borrower.

**(d) Survival.** The indemnities, protections, and exculpatory provisions in favor of the retiring or removed Administrative Agent under this Agreement and the other Loan Documents shall survive its resignation or removal and the termination of this Agreement.

**[Section 8.07 --- Non-Reliance on Administrative Agent and Other Lenders]{.underline}**

Each Lender and the Issuing Bank acknowledges that it has, independently and without reliance upon the Administrative Agent, any other Lender, or any of their respective Related Parties, and based on such documents and information as it has deemed appropriate, made its own credit analysis of the Borrower and its Subsidiaries and its own decision to enter into this Agreement and to make its Loans or participate in Letters of Credit hereunder. Each Lender and the Issuing Bank also acknowledges that it will, independently and without reliance upon the Administrative Agent, any other Lender, or any of their respective Related Parties, and based on such documents and information as it shall from time to time deem appropriate, continue to make its own decisions in taking or not taking action under or based upon this Agreement, any other Loan Document, or any related agreement or any document furnished hereunder or thereunder.

**[Section 8.08 --- Administrative Agent May File Proofs of Claim]{.underline}**

In case of the pendency of any proceeding under any Debtor Relief Law or any other judicial proceeding relative to the Borrower or any other Loan Party, the Administrative Agent (irrespective of whether the principal of any Loan or any reimbursement obligation in respect of any Letter of Credit shall then be due and payable as herein expressed or by declaration or otherwise and irrespective of whether the Administrative Agent shall have made any demand on the Borrower) shall be entitled and empowered, by intervention in such proceeding or otherwise:

> \(a\) to file and prove a claim for the whole amount of the principal and interest owing and unpaid in respect of the Loans, reimbursement obligations in respect of Letters of Credit, and all other

**[SECOND LIEN CREDIT AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**dated as of July 31, 2025**

**among**

**\$150,000,000 Second Lien Term Loan B Facility**

*Arranged by Ridgeline Capital Partners, LP*

**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"Borrower\"), has requested that the Lenders (as defined herein) provide a second lien term loan facility in an aggregate principal amount of One Hundred Fifty Million Dollars (\$150,000,000) (the \"Second Lien Term Loan B Facility\") to refinance certain existing indebtedness of the Borrower and for general corporate purposes;

**WHEREAS**, the Lenders are willing to extend such credit to the Borrower on the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in this Agreement;

**WHEREAS**, concurrently with the execution and delivery of this Agreement, the Borrower, the lenders party thereto, and Cartwright National Bank, N.A., as administrative agent, are entering into that certain First Lien Credit Agreement dated as of July 31, 2025 (the \"First Lien Credit Agreement\"), providing for first lien secured credit facilities in an aggregate principal amount of Four Hundred Seventy-Five Million Dollars (\$475,000,000);

**WHEREAS**, the proceeds of the Term Loans made hereunder, together with the proceeds of loans made under the First Lien Credit Agreement, will be used to refinance certain existing indebtedness of the Borrower and for general corporate purposes; and

**WHEREAS**, capitalized terms used in these Recitals and not otherwise defined herein shall have the meanings ascribed to such terms in Article I of this Agreement.

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements herein contained, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties hereto agree as follows:
